code: # Build a minimal design with 1 sampled day per stratum dates_1psu <- c(as.Date("2024-06-03"), as.Date("2024-06-08")) cal_1psu <- data.frame(date = dates_1psu, day_type = c("weekday", "weekend")) d_1psu <- creel_design(cal_1psu, date = date, strata = day_type) counts_1psu <- data.frame(   date     = dates_1psu,   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   count    = c(10L, 15L) ) d_1psu <- add_counts(d_1psu, counts_1psu)  # Taylor linearization raises an error when variance cannot be estimated estimate_effort(d_1psu, variance = "jackknife") #> Error in `value[[3L]]()`: #> ! Stratum "weekday" has only 1 PSU — jackknife variance cannot be #>   estimated. #> ✖ A stratum must have at least 2 PSUs for jackknife resampling. #> ℹ Increase the sampling rate for stratum "weekday", or use `variance = #>   'taylor'`.

title: Variance Methods: Taylor, Bootstrap, and Jackknife
text: Variance method comparison

title: Spatially Stratified Estimation with Sections
text: spatially stratified creel survey divides lake discrete geographic sections — example, North, Central, South — managed separate reporting unit. section observed count data set angler interviews, effort levels catch rates can differ materially sections. add_sections() right tool survey design explicitly stratifies section: different field crews patrol different areas, section-level estimates required final report, lake large enough assuming uniform effort across full water body misleading. simply curious spatial patterns design survey sections mind, use standard creel_design() workflow without add_sections().

title: Spatially Stratified Estimation with Sections
text: design now shows three registered sections (North, Central, South). downstream estimators automatically detect sections return per-section output.
code: library(tidycreel)  data(example_sections_calendar) data(example_sections_counts) data(example_sections_interviews)  sections_df <- data.frame(   section = c("North", "Central", "South"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  design <- creel_design(example_sections_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- add_sections(design, sections_df, section_col = section) design <- add_counts(design, example_sections_counts) design <- add_interviews(   design, example_sections_interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   harvest = catch_kept,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 27 interviews: 27 complete (100%), 0 incomplete (0%) print(design) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 12 days (2024-06-03 to 2024-06-21) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: 36 observations #> PSU column: date #> Count type: "instantaneous" #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Interviews: 27 observations #> Type: "access" #> Catch: catch_total #> Effort: hours_fished #> Harvest: catch_kept #> Trip status: 27 complete, 0 incomplete #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Sections: 3 registered #> North #> Central #> South

title: Spatially Stratified Estimation with Sections
text: estimate_effort() detects registered sections returns one row per section plus .lake_total row aggregates across sections. lake-total standard error accounts cross-section covariance (described Variance Aggregation section ). Central highest fishing effort among three sections, South lowest. prop_of_lake_total column shows section’s share total angler-hours survey period.
code: effort_est <- estimate_effort(design) print(effort_est$estimates) #> # A tibble: 4 × 10 #>   section     estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1 North            269 12.3       12.3          0    242.      296.    12 #> 2 Central          472 19.0       19.0          0    430.      514.    12 #> 3 South            105  9.18       9.18         0     84.6     125.    12 #> 4 .lake_total      846 39.4       NA           NA    758.      934.    36 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: prop_of_lake_total <dbl>, data_available <lgl>

title: Spatially Stratified Estimation with Sections
text: Catch rate (CPUE, fish per angler-hour) ratio estimator. Ratios additive across sections: average North’s rate South’s rate produce valid lake-wide CPUE. reason, estimate_catch_rate() sectioned design returns one row per section .lake_total row. Notice .lake_total row. Catch rate ratio estimator ratios additive — South’s high catch rate simply averaged North’s low rate produce valid lake-wide CPUE. estimate lake-wide catch rate, call estimate_catch_rate() design without section registration.
code: cpue_est <- estimate_catch_rate(design) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=27, 100% of 27 interviews) [default] print(cpue_est$estimates) #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   section estimate     se ci_lower ci_upper     n data_available #>   <chr>      <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> <lgl>          #> 1 North       1.06 0.0710    0.922     1.20     9 TRUE           #> 2 Central     1.51 0.0179    1.47      1.54     9 TRUE           #> 3 South       2.45 0.0287    2.39      2.51     9 TRUE # Lake-wide catch rate: build an unsectioned design using the same data design_nosections <- creel_design(example_sections_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design_nosections <- add_counts(design_nosections, example_sections_counts) design_nosections <- add_interviews(   design_nosections, example_sections_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished,   harvest = catch_kept, trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 27 interviews: 27 complete (100%), 0 incomplete (0%) estimate_catch_rate(design_nosections)$estimates #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=27, 100% of 27 interviews) [default] #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     1.77 0.118     1.54     2.00    27

title: Spatially Stratified Estimation with Sections
text: Total catch combines effort catch rate within section: TC_i = E_i × CPUE_i. lake-wide total sum(TC_i) — E_total × CPUE_pooled. aggregate_sections = TRUE (default), .lake_total row appended output. South high catch rate lowest effort, limits contribution lake total. Central moderate catch rate combined highest effort, making largest contributor. prop_of_lake_total column shows section’s percentage contribution lake-wide total catch.
code: catch_est <- estimate_total_catch(design, aggregate_sections = TRUE) print(catch_est$estimates) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #>   section     estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n prop_of_lake_total #>   <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int>              <dbl> #> 1 North           285.  23.1     240.     331.     9              0.228 #> 2 Central         711.  29.9     653.     770.     9              0.567 #> 3 South           257.  22.7     213.     302.     9              0.205 #> 4 .lake_total    1254.  44.1    1156.    1352.     3              1     #> # ℹ 1 more variable: data_available <lgl> harvest_est <- estimate_total_harvest(design, aggregate_sections = TRUE) print(harvest_est$estimates) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #>   section     estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n prop_of_lake_total #>   <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int>              <dbl> #> 1 North           165.  15.8     134.     196.     9              0.204 #> 2 Central         466.  23.8     419.     512.     9              0.576 #> 3 South           178.  15.9     147.     210.     9              0.221 #> 4 .lake_total     809.  32.7     736.     882.     3              1     #> # ℹ 1 more variable: data_available <lgl>

what: Variance Aggregation — Why method = "correlated" Is the Default
title: Spatially Stratified Estimation with Sections
text: standard shared-calendar creel design, field crew works entire lake survey day. Every section counted interviewed calendar days: day sampled, three sections observed; day sampled, none . Sharing survey days creates cross-section covariance sampling errors. practice covariance tends negative: high-effort days sections run high together, low-effort days run low together. shared-calendar variance formula accounts covariance produces narrower lake-total standard error simply adding section standard errors quadrature. method = "independent" appropriate sections surveyed separate, uncoordinated crews — example, South section run Crew different days North section (Crew B). case sections truly independent sampling errors Cochran (1977, §5.2) additivity applies: SE_total = sqrt(sum(SE_h^2)). Rule thumb: crew drives entire lake survey day, use default method = "correlated". different sections separate, non-overlapping calendars, use method = "independent".
code: # Default: accounts for cross-section covariance (shared-calendar designs) estimate_effort(design, method = "correlated")$estimates #> # A tibble: 4 × 10 #>   section     estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1 North            269 12.3       12.3          0    242.      296.    12 #> 2 Central          472 19.0       19.0          0    430.      514.    12 #> 3 South            105  9.18       9.18         0     84.6     125.    12 #> 4 .lake_total      846 39.4       NA           NA    758.      934.    36 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: prop_of_lake_total <dbl>, data_available <lgl>  # Alternative: Cochran 5.2 additivity (independent crews, non-overlapping calendars) estimate_effort(design, method = "independent")$estimates #> # A tibble: 4 × 10 #>   section     estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1 North            269 12.3       12.3          0    242.      296.    12 #> 2 Central          472 19.0       19.0          0    430.      514.    12 #> 3 South            105  9.18       9.18         0     84.6     125.    12 #> 4 .lake_total      846 24.4       NA           NA    792.      900.    36 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: prop_of_lake_total <dbl>, data_available <lgl>

title: Spatially Stratified Estimation with Sections
text: registered section count data survey day, estimate_effort() produces NA row data_available = FALSE emits warning. prevents silent omission sections observed. turn warnings errors (automated pipelines), use missing_sections = "error".
code: north_central_counts <- example_sections_counts[   example_sections_counts$section != "South", ]  design_missing <- creel_design(example_sections_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design_missing <- add_sections(design_missing, sections_df, section_col = section) design_missing <- suppressWarnings(add_counts(design_missing, north_central_counts))  estimate_effort(design_missing, missing_sections = "warn")$estimates #> Warning: 1 missing section(s) in count data. #> ! Section(s) not found: "South" #> ℹ Inserting NA row(s) with data_available = FALSE. #> # A tibble: 4 × 10 #>   section     estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1 North            269  12.3       12.3         0     242.     296.    12 #> 2 Central          472  19.0       19.0         0     430.     514.    12 #> 3 South             NA  NA         NA          NA      NA       NA      0 #> 4 .lake_total      741  31.1       NA          NA     672.     810.    24 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: prop_of_lake_total <dbl>, data_available <lgl>

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: well-structured sampling schedule ensures creel survey estimates effort, catch, CPUE unbiased representative full survey season. generate_schedule() builds stratified random sampling calendar season boundaries target sampling intensity — producing count-period framework drives tidycreel estimation pipeline.

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: Every tidycreel design rests calendar count periods — discrete time slots field crews collect data. count period defined : Date: survey date Period: time--day window (e.g., “Morning”, “Afternoon”) Stratum: day type (weekday vs. weekend) Interviews angler counts collected count periods. schedule generate defines exactly days periods sampled.

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: minimal inputs season boundaries, number periods per day, sampling intensity. example builds schedule May–September creel season morning afternoon count periods, sampling 30% weekdays 50% weekends.
code: library(tidycreel)  sched <- generate_schedule(   start_date    = "2024-05-01",   end_date      = "2024-09-30",   n_periods     = 2,   period_labels = c("Morning", "Afternoon"),   sampling_rate = c(weekday = 0.30, weekend = 0.50),   seed          = 42 )  head(sched, 8)

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: result creel_schedule object date, day_type, period_id columns. Period labels replace integer IDs human-readable names.
code: # Counts of scheduled days by stratum table(sched$day_type) #>  #> weekday weekend  #>      66      44

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: Pass include_all = TRUE return every day season sampled indicator. useful checking seasonal coverage communicating design stakeholders. sampled days drawn stratified random sampling within stratum, weekday weekend sampling fractions match requested rates.
code: sched_full <- generate_schedule(   start_date    = "2024-05-01",   end_date      = "2024-09-30",   n_periods     = 1,   sampling_rate = c(weekday = 0.30, weekend = 0.50),   include_all   = TRUE,   seed          = 42 )  # Sampled proportion by stratum table(sched_full$day_type, sched_full$sampled) #>           #>           FALSE TRUE #>   weekday    76   33 #>   weekend    22   22

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: surveys divide day three periods — morning, midday, evening — capture temporal patterns angler activity (e.g., morning evening peak low midday use).
code: sched3 <- generate_schedule(   start_date    = "2024-06-01",   end_date      = "2024-08-31",   n_periods     = 3,   period_labels = c("Morning", "Midday", "Evening"),   sampling_rate = c(weekday = 0.40, weekend = 0.60),   seed          = 101 )  head(sched3, 9)

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: seed argument ensures calendar produced every time. important schedule pre-printed field use must match estimators use.
code: sched_a <- generate_schedule("2024-06-01", "2024-06-30",   n_periods = 1,   sampling_rate = 0.4, seed = 99 ) sched_b <- generate_schedule("2024-06-01", "2024-06-30",   n_periods = 1,   sampling_rate = 0.4, seed = 99 )  identical(sched_a, sched_b) #> [1] TRUE

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: write_schedule() exports CSV (spreadsheet application) xlsx (direct field printing). read_schedule() reloads file correct column types, schedule round-trips without type coercion issues.
code: # Save for field crews write_schedule(sched, "count_schedule_2024.csv") write_schedule(sched, "count_schedule_2024.xlsx")  # Reload — column types are preserved automatically sched_reload <- read_schedule("count_schedule_2024.csv") identical(sched, sched_reload)

what: Linking the Schedule to a Design
title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: Pass schedule directly creel_design(). schedule’s stratum structure informs survey weights used downstream estimators.
code: design <- creel_design(sched, date = date, strata = day_type) design #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 110 days (2024-05-03 to 2024-09-30) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: "none" #> Interviews: "none" #> Sections: "none"

what: Count Period Scheduling vs. Interview Scheduling
title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: standard access-point creel surveys, interviews happen count periods — field crew counts anglers also approaches interviews. count schedule therefore governs collection activities. separate “interview schedule.” set available interviews set interviews collected sampled count-period days.

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: bus-route designs, generate_bus_schedule() converts count schedule site-level sampling frame complete bus-route sampling frame inclusion probabilities. site’s probability visited given period : πi=psite×pperiodwherepperiod=crewncircuits\pi_i = p_{site} \times p_{period} \quad \text{} \quad p_{period} = \frac{\text{crew}}{n_{circuits}} inclusion_prob column per-site overall inclusion probability across season. frame passes directly creel_design(survey_type = "bus_route", ...).
code: # Site-level selection probabilities (must sum to 1.0) site_frame <- data.frame(   site_id = c("North Bay", "South Cove", "Dock Area", "Main Channel"),   p_site  = c(0.35, 0.25, 0.25, 0.15) )  # Build bus-route sampling frame using the count schedule bus_frame <- generate_bus_schedule(   schedule       = sched,   sampling_frame = site_frame,   site           = site_id,   p_site         = p_site,   crew           = 1 )  bus_frame #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   site_id      p_site p_period inclusion_prob #>   <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 North Bay      0.35        1           0.35 #> 2 South Cove     0.25        1           0.25 #> 3 Dock Area      0.25        1           0.25 #> 4 Main Channel   0.15        1           0.15

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: right sampling intensity depends target precision survey. starting point: Higher rates weekends reflect greater angler activity higher stratum variance. Use creel_n_effort() creel_power() select rates based CV target rather rule thumb.
code: # Effort sample size to achieve CV ≤ 0.15 # N_h: total days per stratum in a 184-day season # ybar_h: pilot mean daily effort (angler-hours) per stratum # s2_h: pilot variance of daily effort per stratum creel_n_effort(   cv_target = 0.15,   N_h = c(weekday = 132, weekend = 52),   ybar_h = c(weekday = 280, weekend = 550),   s2_h = c(weekday = 14400, weekend = 32400) ) #> weekday weekend   total  #>       6       2       7

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: Counts primary effort estimator creel surveys: number anglers observed count window used estimate total angler-hours. count windows fall day matters — clustered poorly spaced windows can introduce bias effort estimates. Pollock et al. (1994) describe three strategies placing count time windows within survey day: random, systematic, fixed. generate_count_times() implements three.

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: random strategy, n_windows non-overlapping windows drawn uniformly random available survey hours. window_size sets duration (minutes) window min_gap enforces minimum separation (minutes) consecutive windows prevent back--back counts. (date column render calendar) n_windows total number count windows place day, window_size long window lasts minutes, min_gap minimum idle time end one window start next.
code: library(tidycreel) ct_random <- generate_count_times(   start_time  = "06:00",   end_time    = "14:00",   strategy    = "random",   n_windows   = 4,   window_size = 30,   min_gap     = 10,   seed        = 42 ) ct_random

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: systematic strategy uses Pollock et al. (1994) model: day divided n_windows equal-length strata one window placed random start within first stratum; subsequent windows begin t₁+k, t₁+2k, , k stratum length. ensures even spacing across day. Systematic sampling preferred many agencies (Colorado CPW 2012) avoids clustered count times can occur chance random placement. (date column render calendar)
code: ct_systematic <- generate_count_times(   start_time  = "06:00",   end_time    = "14:00",   strategy    = "systematic",   n_windows   = 4,   window_size = 30,   min_gap     = 10,   seed        = 42 ) ct_systematic

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: Use fixed strategy count time windows defined regulation, permit conditions, prior-season protocol must replicated exactly. Supply data.frame start_time end_time columns generate_count_times() wraps creel_schedule object without random placement. (date column render calendar)
code: fw <- data.frame(   start_time = c("07:00", "09:30", "12:00"),   end_time = c("07:30", "10:00", "12:30"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ct_fixed <- generate_count_times(strategy = "fixed", fixed_windows = fw) ct_fixed

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: generate_count_times() returns creel_schedule object, class returned generate_schedule(). therefore passes directly write_schedule() field printing without conversion step.
code: write_schedule(ct_systematic, "count_times_2024.csv")

what: Combining the Daily Schedule with Count Time Windows
title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: generate_schedule() generate_count_times() return separate creel_schedule. field dispatch, biologists need single table — one row per (date x period x count window) — tells crew exactly count. attach_count_times() performs cross-join one step.
code: sched <- generate_schedule(   start_date = "2024-06-01", end_date = "2024-06-07",   n_periods = 2,   sampling_rate = c(weekday = 0.5, weekend = 0.8),   seed = 42 ) ct <- generate_count_times(   start_time = "06:00", end_time = "14:00",   strategy = "systematic", n_windows = 3,   window_size = 30, min_gap = 10,   seed = 42 ) field_schedule <- attach_count_times(sched, ct) field_schedule

previous_headings: Combining the Daily Schedule with Count Time Windows
title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: result nrow(sched) * nrow(ct) rows — every sampled day-period combination appears per count window. Pass field_schedule directly write_schedule() produce printable field dispatch sheet.

what: Validating the Design Before the Season
title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: building schedule, validate_design() checks whether proposed sampling intensity sufficient meet target coefficient variation (CV) — catching -sampling problems season starts rather discovering post-season analysis. uses pilot mean variance inputs creel_n_effort() compares proposed day counts minimum required sample size per stratum. report$results shows per-stratum status (pass / warn / fail), proposed sample size, required sample size, achieved CV proposed intensity. report$passed TRUE strata pass — use go/-go gate printing field schedules.
code: report <- validate_design(   N_h        = c(weekday = 132, weekend = 52),   ybar_h     = c(weekday = 280, weekend = 550),   s2_h       = c(weekday = 14400, weekend = 32400),   n_proposed = c(weekday = 40L, weekend = 26L),   cv_target  = 0.15 ) report #>  #> ── Design Validation Report ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: effort #> ✔ All strata PASSED #>  #> ✔ weekday: n=40 >= 6 required (CV 0.006 vs target 0.15) #> ✔ weekend: n=26 >= 2 required (CV 0.009 vs target 0.15) report$results #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   stratum status n_proposed n_required cv_actual cv_target message               #>   <chr>   <chr>       <int>      <int>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>                 #> 1 weekday pass           40          6    0.0059      0.15 Proposed n meets or … #> 2 weekend pass           26          2    0.0089      0.15 Proposed n meets or …

what: Checking Data Completeness After the Season
title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: check_completeness() runs post-season creel_design survey data attached, flagging missing sampling days strata interviews produce reliable estimates. first diagnostic step running estimators — resolving completeness issues early avoids propagating gaps effort CPUE estimates. comp$passed returns TRUE issues found. FALSE, comp$missing_days identifies dates appear schedule count data, comp$low_n_strata identifies strata interview count minimum threshold (n_min, default 10).
code: data(example_calendar) data(example_counts) data(example_interviews)  design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- add_counts(design, example_counts) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability design <- add_interviews(design, example_interviews,   catch        = catch_total,   effort       = hours_fished,   trip_status  = trip_status ) comp <- check_completeness(design) comp #>  #> ── Completeness Report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Survey type: instantaneous | n_min threshold: 10 #> ✖ Completeness issues found #>  #>  #> ── Missing Days ── #>  #> ✔ No missing sampling days #>  #> ── Low-n Strata (threshold: 10) ── #>  #> ! 1 stratum/strata below n_min=10 #>  #> ── Refusal Rates ── #>  #> (not recorded or not applicable)

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: season_summary() accepts named list creel_estimates objects — outputs estimate_effort(), estimate_catch_rate(), related functions — joins single wide tibble reporting export. performs re-estimation; purely assembly step makes easy view key metrics side side. full estimation workflow — building design, attaching counts interviews, running estimators — covered main tidycreel vignette. season_summary() final step wraps pre-computed results export reporting. write_schedule() accepts data frame tibble, season summary table exports CSV xlsx single call.
code: # Run estimators first (see the tidycreel workflow vignette) effort <- estimate_effort(design) cpue <- estimate_catch_rate(design)  # Assemble the season summary summary_tbl <- season_summary(list(effort = effort, cpue = cpue)) summary_tbl$table write_schedule(summary_tbl$table, "season_2024_summary.xlsx")

title: Survey Scheduling: Count and Interview Periods
text: Hoenig, J. M., Robson, D. S., Jones, C. M., Pollock, K. H. (1993). Scheduling counts instantaneous progressive count methods estimating sportfishing effort. North American Journal Fisheries Management, 13, 723–736. Pollock, K. H., Jones, C. M., Brown, T. L. (1994). Angler Survey Methods Applications Fisheries Management. American Fisheries Society, Bethesda, MD.

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: tidycreel package built top R’s survey package. Every estimation function tidycreel — estimate_effort(), estimate_catch_rate(), estimate_total_catch() — wrapper around corresponding survey package call. design objects tidycreel constructs standard svydesign objects hood, estimators delegate directly svymean(), svytotal(), svyratio(). vignette walks effort + catch rate + total catch analysis twice: first using raw survey package calls, using tidycreel. goal make mapping explicit, R users familiar survey package can immediately understand tidycreel . already know svyratio() delta method variance propagation, recognize computations tidycreel’s output.

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: workflows use three example datasets included tidycreel. Load shared across parts vignette. calendar covers 14 days (10 weekdays, 4 weekend days). counts record observed effort (angler-hours) sampled day. interviews record catch effort individual completed incomplete trips.
code: library(tidycreel)  data(example_calendar) data(example_counts) data(example_interviews)  head(example_calendar) #>         date day_type #> 1 2024-06-01  weekend #> 2 2024-06-02  weekend #> 3 2024-06-03  weekday #> 4 2024-06-04  weekday #> 5 2024-06-05  weekday #> 6 2024-06-06  weekday head(example_counts) #>         date day_type effort_hours #> 1 2024-06-01  weekend         45.2 #> 2 2024-06-02  weekend         52.8 #> 3 2024-06-03  weekday         12.5 #> 4 2024-06-04  weekday         18.3 #> 5 2024-06-05  weekday         15.7 #> 6 2024-06-06  weekday         22.1 head(example_interviews) #>         date hours_fished catch_total catch_kept trip_status trip_duration #> 1 2024-06-01          2.0           5          2    complete           2.0 #> 2 2024-06-01          3.5           8          5    complete           3.5 #> 3 2024-06-02          1.5           2          1    complete           1.5 #> 4 2024-06-02          2.0           3          2  incomplete           1.0 #> 5 2024-06-03          2.5           6          3    complete           2.5 #> 6 2024-06-03          4.0          12          8    complete           4.0 #>   interview_id angler_type angler_method species_sought n_anglers refused #> 1            1        bank          bait        walleye         2   FALSE #> 2            2        boat    artificial        walleye         1   FALSE #> 3            3        bank          bait           bass         3   FALSE #> 4            4        bank           fly        panfish         2   FALSE #> 5            5        boat    artificial        walleye         1   FALSE #> 6            6        boat          bait           bass         4   FALSE

what: 1a. Survey Design from a Stratified Calendar
title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: creel survey weekday/weekend stratification stratified simple random sample. finite population correction (FPC) stratum total number days stratum survey period. example_counts already carries day_type column (copied calendar), can build FPC column directly calendar frequency table. 14 survey days observed (census 14-day period), FPC reduces 1 standard error totals 0. real survey subset days sampled, FPC less 1 variances positive.
code: library(survey) #> Loading required package: grid #> Loading required package: Matrix #> Loading required package: survival #>  #> Attaching package: 'survey' #> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics': #>  #>     dotchart  # Stratum sizes: total days per day_type in the calendar stratum_sizes <- table(example_calendar$day_type) stratum_sizes #>  #> weekday weekend  #>      10       4  # Attach FPC to the count frame counts_frame <- example_counts counts_frame$fpc <- as.integer(stratum_sizes[counts_frame$day_type]) counts_frame #>          date day_type effort_hours fpc #> 1  2024-06-01  weekend         45.2   4 #> 2  2024-06-02  weekend         52.8   4 #> 3  2024-06-03  weekday         12.5  10 #> 4  2024-06-04  weekday         18.3  10 #> 5  2024-06-05  weekday         15.7  10 #> 6  2024-06-06  weekday         22.1  10 #> 7  2024-06-07  weekday         14.9  10 #> 8  2024-06-08  weekend         48.6   4 #> 9  2024-06-09  weekend         55.3   4 #> 10 2024-06-10  weekday         16.8  10 #> 11 2024-06-11  weekday         19.4  10 #> 12 2024-06-12  weekday         13.2  10 #> 13 2024-06-13  weekday         17.6  10 #> 14 2024-06-14  weekday         20.1  10  # Build the stratified survey design svy_counts <- svydesign(   ids    = ~1,   strata = ~day_type,   fpc    = ~fpc,   data   = counts_frame ) svy_counts #> Stratified Independent Sampling design #> svydesign(ids = ~1, strata = ~day_type, fpc = ~fpc, data = counts_frame)

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: Total effort sum observed angler-hours across 14 days, estimated via svytotal() count frame design. result estimated total angler-hours survey period, 95% confidence interval. example uses complete census 14 days, standard error 0. practice, creel surveys sample subset days, svytotal() extrapolates observed days full survey period using stratum weights.
code: effort_total <- svytotal(~effort_hours, svy_counts) effort_total #>              total SE #> effort_hours 372.5  0 confint(effort_total) #>              2.5 % 97.5 % #> effort_hours 372.5  372.5

what: 1c. Catch Rate via svyratio and Total Catch via the Delta Method
title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: Catch per unit effort (CPUE) estimated interview data using ratio--means estimator: total catch across complete interviews divided total effort across interviews. svyratio() approach. CPUE estimate ratio total catch total effort across complete interviews. svyratio() function computes Taylor linearization variance ratio, accounting correlation catch effort within interview. Now combine effort total CPUE using delta method propagate variance product E × C:
code:
# Use complete trips only (standard practice) complete_trips <- subset(example_interviews, trip_status == "complete") nrow(complete_trips) #> [1] 17  # Build a simple survey design over the interview frame svy_interviews <- svydesign(ids = ~1, data = complete_trips) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = ~1, data = complete_trips): No weights or #> probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability  # Ratio estimator: total catch / total effort (ratio-of-means CPUE) cpue_ratio <- svyratio(~catch_total, ~hours_fished, svy_interviews) cpue_ratio #> Ratio estimator: svyratio.survey.design2(~catch_total, ~hours_fished, svy_interviews) #> Ratios= #>             hours_fished #> catch_total     2.285714 #> SEs= #>             hours_fished #> catch_total     0.112297 effort_coef <- coef(effort_total)[[1]] cpue_coef <- coef(cpue_ratio)[[1]] var_effort <- vcov(effort_total)[[1]] var_cpue <- vcov(cpue_ratio)[[1]]  # Delta method: Var(effort * cpue) = effort^2 * Var(cpue) + cpue^2 * Var(effort) total_catch_est <- effort_coef * cpue_coef total_catch_var <- effort_coef^2 * var_cpue + cpue_coef^2 * var_effort total_catch_se <- sqrt(total_catch_var)  cat("Total catch estimate:", round(total_catch_est, 1), "
") #> Total catch estimate: 851.4 cat("Standard error:      ", round(total_catch_se, 1), "
") #> Standard error:       41.8 cat(   "95% CI: [",   round(total_catch_est - 1.96 * total_catch_se, 1), ",",   round(total_catch_est + 1.96 * total_catch_se, 1), "]
" ) #> 95% CI: [ 769.4 , 933.4 ]

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: creel_design() wraps calendar stratification design object. add_counts() attaches count frame builds internal svydesign object — equivalent svydesign() call Part 1a . design reports 14 observations across two strata (weekday, weekend) raw svydesign object .
code: design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- add_counts(design, example_counts) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability design #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 14 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-14) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: 14 observations #> PSU column: date #> Count type: "instantaneous" #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Interviews: "none" #> Sections: "none"

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: estimate_effort() calls svytotal() internal design object returns result tidy tibble labelled columns. estimate (372.5 angler-hours) matches raw svytotal() result Part 1b. tidycreel adds CI directly output labels column estimate instead raw column name.
code: effort_est <- estimate_effort(design) effort_est #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     372.  13.2       13.2         0     344.     401.    14

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: calling estimate_catch_rate(), attach interview data via add_interviews(). maps interview columns design vocabulary filters complete trips default. Now estimate CPUE, calls svyratio() internally complete-trip subset interview frame: CPUE estimate (2.29) SE (0.11) match svyratio() output Part 1c.
code: design <- add_interviews(design, example_interviews,   catch         = catch_total,   effort        = hours_fished,   harvest       = catch_kept,   trip_status   = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%) cpue_est <- estimate_catch_rate(design) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=17, 77.3% of 22 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 17. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. cpue_est #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Ratio-of-Means CPUE #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     2.29 0.114     2.06     2.51    17

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: estimate_total_catch() applies delta method combine effort CPUE estimates, exactly Part 1c: total catch estimate (approximately 851) consistent raw delta-method calculation Part 1c. Small numerical differences reflect tidycreel’s internal handling complete-trip filter variance components — statistical method identical.
code: total_catch <- estimate_total_catch(design) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=17, 77.3% of 22 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 17. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. total_catch #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total Catch (Effort × CPUE) #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     851.  52.0     749.     953.    17

title: tidycreel and the survey Package: A Side-by-Side Guide
text: Use tidycreel standard creel survey designs main analysis follows effort + CPUE + total catch workflow. tidycreel handles bookkeeping (FPC construction, complete-trip filtering, delta method variance) returns tidy tibbles ready reporting. domain vocabulary (creel_design, estimate_effort, estimate_catch_rate) matches language creel biologists already use. Use raw survey package calls need capabilities outside tidycreel’s scope: custom clustering structures, probability-proportional--size (PPS) sampling, replicate-weight designs, nonstandard variance estimators. as_survey_design() function extracts internal svydesign object tidycreel design, giving full access survey package toolbox still using tidycreel initial data setup.
code: # Extract the internal svydesign object for advanced use internal_svy <- as_survey_design(design) # Now use any survey package function directly svymean(~effort_hours, internal_svy)

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: Creel surveys sample fraction days season. Design-based estimators tidycreel automatically account unsampled days survey weights — call estimate_effort() estimate_total_catch(), result already season-total estimate, just sample-day sum. vignette shows : Obtain full-season total single design Break season monthly totals using separate monthly designs Combine monthly estimates season annual total Assemble multi-estimate reports season_summary()

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: create design creel_design(), survey object assigns weight sampled day. day sampled rate ff receives weight 1/f1/f, observed angler-hours represent 1/f1/f days stratum type. estimator sums weighted observations across strata produce population total entire design period. practice means: season design covering May–September gives season-total estimate. monthly design covering just June gives June-total estimate. need multiply results “expansion factor” — weights already perform extrapolation.

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
code: library(tidycreel)  set.seed(2024)  # Full season: May 1 – September 30 (184 days) all_dates <- seq(as.Date("2024-05-01"), as.Date("2024-09-30"), by = "1 day") day_types <- ifelse(   as.integer(format(all_dates, "%u")) %in% c(6L, 7L), "weekend", "weekday" )  season_calendar <- data.frame(   date       = all_dates,   day_type   = day_types,   open_hours = 10 )  # Stratified random sample: 30% of weekdays, 50% of weekends set.seed(2024) sampled <- ave(   seq_along(all_dates), day_types,   FUN = function(i) {     rate <- if (day_types[i[1]] == "weekend") 0.50 else 0.30     as.integer(runif(length(i)) < rate)   } ) sampled_calendar <- season_calendar[as.logical(sampled), ]  # Generate plausible effort counts (angler-hours per count) # Weekend counts higher than weekday sampled_counts <- data.frame(   date = sampled_calendar$date,   day_type = sampled_calendar$day_type,   n_anglers = ifelse(     sampled_calendar$day_type == "weekend",     round(rnorm(sum(sampled), mean = 55, sd = 18)),     round(rnorm(sum(sampled), mean = 28, sd = 12))   ) ) sampled_counts$n_anglers <- pmax(sampled_counts$n_anglers, 0L)  nrow(sampled_calendar) # Number of sampled days #> [1] 58

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: Pass sampled calendar creel_design(), attach counts, call estimate_effort(). result estimated total angler-hours entire May–September season. estimate column season-total angler-hours; se standard error total.
code: season_design <- creel_design(   sampled_calendar,   date = date, strata = day_type ) season_design <- add_counts(season_design, sampled_counts) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability  season_effort <- estimate_effort(season_design) season_effort$estimates #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     2004  121.       121.         0    1763.    2245.    58

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: break season monthly totals, subset calendar counts month, build separate monthly design, estimate. Monthly designs independent, variances can combined later.
code: months <- 5:9 month_labels <- c("May", "June", "July", "August", "September")  monthly_effort <- lapply(seq_along(months), function(i) {   m <- months[i]   cal_m <- sampled_calendar[format(sampled_calendar$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]   cnt_m <- sampled_counts[format(sampled_counts$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]    if (nrow(cal_m) == 0) {     return(NULL)   }    des_m <- creel_design(cal_m, date = date, strata = day_type)   des_m <- add_counts(des_m, cnt_m)   est <- estimate_effort(des_m)$estimates    cbind(month = month_labels[i], est) }) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability  do.call(rbind, monthly_effort) #>       month estimate       se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper  n #> 1       May      257 45.19735   45.19735         0 150.1253 363.8747  9 #> 2      June      461 73.10814   73.10814         0 300.0901 621.9099 13 #> 3      July      338 57.00058   57.00058         0 206.5564 469.4436 10 #> 4    August      491 55.13036   55.13036         0 369.6589 612.3411 13 #> 5 September      457 39.73244   39.73244         0 369.5495 544.4505 13

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: monthly estimates independent (non-overlapping time windows), season total sum monthly estimates season variance sum monthly variances. close single-design season total (small differences due independent stratification within monthly design).
code: monthly_df <- do.call(rbind, monthly_effort)  season_from_months <- data.frame(   stratum  = "Season total",   estimate = sum(monthly_df$estimate),   se       = sqrt(sum(monthly_df$se^2)) )  season_from_months #>        stratum estimate       se #> 1 Season total     2004 123.5099

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: Total catch estimation follows monthly pattern: estimate catch rate interviews, multiply monthly effort. First, generate synthetic interview data: loop months exactly effort:
code: set.seed(2024)  # Three interviews per sampled day (ensures ≥10 complete trips per month) n_per_day <- 3L n_int <- nrow(sampled_calendar) * n_per_day catch_total <- rpois(n_int, lambda = 1.8) interviews <- data.frame(   date         = rep(sampled_calendar$date, each = n_per_day),   day_type     = rep(sampled_calendar$day_type, each = n_per_day),   trip_status  = "complete",   hours_fished = round(rnorm(n_int, mean = 3.5, sd = 1.2), 1),   catch_total  = catch_total,   catch_kept   = pmin(rpois(n_int, lambda = 0.6), catch_total) ) interviews$hours_fished <- pmax(interviews$hours_fished, 0.5) monthly_catch <- lapply(seq_along(months), function(i) {   m <- months[i]   cal_m <- sampled_calendar[format(sampled_calendar$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]   cnt_m <- sampled_counts[format(sampled_counts$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]   int_m <- interviews[format(interviews$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]    if (nrow(cal_m) == 0) {     return(NULL)   }    des_m <- creel_design(cal_m, date = date, strata = day_type)   des_m <- add_counts(des_m, cnt_m)   des_m <- add_interviews(des_m, int_m,     trip_status = trip_status,     catch = catch_total,     effort = hours_fished,     harvest = catch_kept   )    effort_est <- estimate_effort(des_m)   catch_est <- estimate_total_catch(des_m)    cbind(month = month_labels[i], catch_est$estimates) }) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 5 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 27. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 7 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 4 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 9 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 7 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data.  do.call(rbind, monthly_catch) #>       month estimate       se  ci_lower ci_upper  n #> 1       May 132.4452 31.63473  70.44225 194.4481 27 #> 2      June 214.1074 47.03040 121.92952 306.2853 39 #> 3      July 146.3074 31.84911  83.88432 208.7305 30 #> 4    August 247.3898 44.49361 160.18390 334.5957 39 #> 5 September 214.4188 34.00801 147.76429 281.0732 39

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: Sum monthly totals:
code: catch_df <- do.call(rbind, monthly_catch) season_catch <- data.frame(   stratum  = "Season total",   estimate = sum(catch_df$estimate),   se       = sqrt(sum(catch_df$se^2)) ) season_catch #>        stratum estimate       se #> 1 Season total 954.6685 85.80913

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: season_summary() assembles multiple creel_estimates objects single wide tibble reporting. monthly summary, build list named entries pass season_summary().
code: # Collect effort and catch estimates for each month into named lists effort_list <- list() catch_list <- list()  for (i in seq_along(months)) {   m <- months[i]   label <- month_labels[i]    cal_m <- sampled_calendar[format(sampled_calendar$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]   cnt_m <- sampled_counts[format(sampled_counts$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]   int_m <- interviews[format(interviews$date, "%m") == sprintf("%02d", m), ]    if (nrow(cal_m) == 0) next    des_m <- creel_design(cal_m, date = date, strata = day_type)   des_m <- add_counts(des_m, cnt_m)   des_m <- add_interviews(des_m, int_m,     trip_status = trip_status,     catch = catch_total,     effort = hours_fished,     harvest = catch_kept   )    effort_list[[label]] <- estimate_effort(des_m)   catch_list[[label]] <- estimate_total_catch(des_m) } #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 5 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 27. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 7 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 4 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 9 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability #> Warning: 7 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data.  # Assemble effort report effort_summary <- season_summary(effort_list) effort_summary$table #> # A tibble: 1 × 35 #>   May_estimate May_se May_se_between May_se_within May_ci_lower May_ci_upper #>          <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1          257   45.2           45.2             0         150.         364. #> # ℹ 29 more variables: May_n <int>, June_estimate <dbl>, June_se <dbl>, #> #   June_se_between <dbl>, June_se_within <dbl>, June_ci_lower <dbl>, #> #   June_ci_upper <dbl>, June_n <int>, July_estimate <dbl>, July_se <dbl>, #> #   July_se_between <dbl>, July_se_within <dbl>, July_ci_lower <dbl>, #> #   July_ci_upper <dbl>, July_n <int>, August_estimate <dbl>, August_se <dbl>, #> #   August_se_between <dbl>, August_se_within <dbl>, August_ci_lower <dbl>, #> #   August_ci_upper <dbl>, August_n <int>, September_estimate <dbl>, … catch_summary <- season_summary(catch_list) catch_summary$table #> # A tibble: 1 × 25 #>   May_estimate May_se May_ci_lower May_ci_upper May_n June_estimate June_se #>          <dbl>  <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> <int>         <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1         132.   31.6         70.4         194.    27          214.    47.0 #> # ℹ 18 more variables: June_ci_lower <dbl>, June_ci_upper <dbl>, June_n <int>, #> #   July_estimate <dbl>, July_se <dbl>, July_ci_lower <dbl>, #> #   July_ci_upper <dbl>, July_n <int>, August_estimate <dbl>, August_se <dbl>, #> #   August_ci_lower <dbl>, August_ci_upper <dbl>, August_n <int>, #> #   September_estimate <dbl>, September_se <dbl>, September_ci_lower <dbl>, #> #   September_ci_upper <dbl>, September_n <int>

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: survey data span multiple calendar years, apply pattern year level: build one design per year, run estimators, sum totals combine variances.
code: # Conceptual pattern — replace with actual data per year year_effort <- list()  for (yr in c(2022, 2023, 2024)) {   cal_yr <- subset(full_calendar, format(date, "%Y") == yr)   cnt_yr <- subset(full_counts, format(date, "%Y") == yr)    des_yr <- creel_design(cal_yr, date = date, strata = day_type)   des_yr <- add_counts(des_yr, cnt_yr)    year_effort[[as.character(yr)]] <- estimate_effort(des_yr) }  # Multi-year report table season_summary(year_effort)$table

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: write_schedule() accepts data frame, can export assembled summary table directly:
code: write_schedule(effort_summary$table, "effort_by_month_2024.csv") write_schedule(effort_summary$table, "effort_by_month_2024.xlsx")

title: Temporal Extrapolation: Monthly, Seasonal, and Annual Estimates
text: Cochran, W. G. (1977). Sampling Techniques, 3rd ed. Wiley, New York. Pollock, K. H., Jones, C. M., Brown, T. L. (1994). Angler Survey Methods Applications Fisheries Management. American Fisheries Society, Bethesda, MD. Su, Y.-S., Liu, P. (2025). Flexible creel survey estimators. Canadian Journal Fisheries Aquatic Sciences, 82, 1–27.

title: Getting Started with tidycreel
text: tidycreel package provides tidy, pipe-friendly interface creel survey design analysis. allows fisheries biologists work domain vocabulary (dates, strata, counts, effort) without needing understand internals survey package. workflow follows simple three-step pattern: Design: Define survey calendar stratification Data: Attach count observations design Estimation: Compute effort estimates variance

title: Getting Started with tidycreel
text: start loading package example calendar dataset: creel_design() function uses tidy selectors, can specify columns name without quotes. design object captures survey structure: 14 days weekday/weekend stratification.
code: library(tidycreel)  # Load example calendar data data(example_calendar) head(example_calendar) #>         date day_type #> 1 2024-06-01  weekend #> 2 2024-06-02  weekend #> 3 2024-06-03  weekday #> 4 2024-06-04  weekday #> 5 2024-06-05  weekday #> 6 2024-06-06  weekday  # Create a creel design with weekday/weekend strata design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) print(design) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 14 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-14) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: "none" #> Interviews: "none" #> Sections: "none"

title: Getting Started with tidycreel
text: Next, attach instantaneous count observations design: add_counts() function validates count data matches design structure, constructs internal survey design object. Notice design now shows count data attached 14 observations.
code: # Load example count data data(example_counts) head(example_counts) #>         date day_type effort_hours #> 1 2024-06-01  weekend         45.2 #> 2 2024-06-02  weekend         52.8 #> 3 2024-06-03  weekday         12.5 #> 4 2024-06-04  weekday         18.3 #> 5 2024-06-05  weekday         15.7 #> 6 2024-06-06  weekday         22.1  # Attach counts to the design design <- add_counts(design, example_counts) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability print(design) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 14 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-14) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: 14 observations #> PSU column: date #> Count type: "instantaneous" #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Interviews: "none" #> Sections: "none"

title: Getting Started with tidycreel
text: count data attached, can estimate total effort across entire survey period: result shows estimated total effort, standard error, 95% confidence interval. estimate approximately 358 angler-hours 14-day period.
code: # Estimate total effort result <- estimate_effort(design) print(result) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     372.  13.2       13.2         0     344.     401.    14

title: Getting Started with tidycreel
text: can also compute estimates separately stratum group using parameter: grouped results show separate estimates weekday weekend periods. Notice weekend effort (approximately 250 hours) much higher weekday effort (approximately 108 hours), reflecting typical recreational fishing patterns. parameter accepts tidy selectors, can group multiple columns use tidyselect helpers like starts_with().
code: # Estimate effort by day_type result_by_day <- estimate_effort(design, by = day_type) print(result_by_day) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #> Grouped by: day_type #>  #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   day_type estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 weekday      171.  9.67       9.67         0     150.     192.    10 #> 2 weekend      202.  8.95       8.95         0     182.     221.     4

title: Getting Started with tidycreel
text: default, estimate_effort() uses Taylor linearization variance estimation. package also supports bootstrap jackknife methods: use method: Taylor linearization (default): Computationally efficient appropriate smooth statistics. recommended default. Bootstrap: Use working non-smooth statistics want verify Taylor linearization assumptions. computationally intensive. Jackknife: Alternative resampling method deterministic (unlike bootstrap). Useful verification bootstrap slow. three methods work grouped estimation well:
code: # Bootstrap variance estimation (500 replicates) set.seed(123) # For reproducibility result_boot <- estimate_effort(design, variance = "bootstrap") print(result_boot) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Bootstrap #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     372.  13.5       13.5         0     343.     402.    14  # Jackknife variance estimation result_jk <- estimate_effort(design, variance = "jackknife") print(result_jk) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Jackknife #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     372.  13.2       13.2         0     344.     401.    14 # Grouped estimation with bootstrap variance set.seed(123) result_grouped_boot <- estimate_effort(design, by = day_type, variance = "bootstrap") print(result_grouped_boot) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Bootstrap #> Confidence level: 95% #> Grouped by: day_type #>  #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   day_type estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 weekday      171. 10.3       10.3          0     148.     193.    10 #> 2 weekend      202.  8.78       8.78         0     183.     221.     4

title: Getting Started with tidycreel
text: vignette covers core tidycreel workflow instantaneous count surveys. details specific functions, see help pages: ?creel_design - Define survey calendar stratification ?add_counts - Attach count data design ?estimate_effort - Compute effort estimates variance ?as_survey_design - Extract internal survey object advanced use information example datasets: ?example_calendar - Example survey calendar ?example_counts - Example count observations

title: Unextrapolated Interview Summaries
text: Creel surveys produce two types summary products: unextrapolated summaries extrapolated estimates. vignette covers unextrapolated summaries — raw tabulations interview records describe composition interviewed parties without applying survey-design weighting. use unextrapolated summaries: Describing interviewed (angler type, method, species sought) Checking refusals participation rates Examining trip-length distributions Computing catch--sought harvest--sought rates quality indicators Important caveat: unextrapolated functions interview-weighted, pressure-weighted. day many anglers interviews weight day anglers many interviews. pressure-weighted, design-correct estimates, use estimate_catch_rate(), estimate_total_catch(), related functions described “Interview-Based Catch Estimation” vignette.

title: Unextrapolated Interview Summaries
text: Start assembling complete design using v0.5.0 data layers: print() output shows five data layers: calendar, counts, interviews, catch, lengths.
code: library(tidycreel)  # Load example datasets data(example_calendar) data(example_counts) data(example_interviews) data(example_catch) data(example_lengths)  # Step 1: Define the survey calendar and stratification design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  # Step 2: Attach instantaneous count observations design <- add_counts(design, example_counts) #> Warning in svydesign.default(ids = psu_formula, strata = strata_formula, : No #> weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability  # Step 3: Attach interview records (all extended v0.5.0 fields) design <- add_interviews(design, example_interviews,   catch          = catch_total,   effort         = hours_fished,   harvest        = catch_kept,   trip_status    = trip_status,   trip_duration  = trip_duration,   angler_type    = angler_type,   angler_method  = angler_method,   species_sought = species_sought,   n_anglers      = n_anglers,   refused        = refused )  # Step 4: Attach species-level catch data design <- add_catch(design, example_catch,   catch_uid     = interview_id,   interview_uid = interview_id,   species       = species,   count         = count,   catch_type    = catch_type )  # Step 5: Attach fish length data (individual harvest + binned release) design <- add_lengths(design, example_lengths,   length_uid     = interview_id,   interview_uid  = interview_id,   species        = species,   length         = length,   length_type    = length_type,   count          = count,   release_format = "binned" )  print(design) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 14 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-14) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: 14 observations #> PSU column: date #> Count type: "instantaneous" #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Interviews: 22 observations #> Type: "access" #> Catch: catch_total #> Effort: hours_fished #> Harvest: catch_kept #> Trip status: 17 complete, 5 incomplete #> Angler type: angler_type #> Angler method: angler_method #> Species sought: species_sought #> Party size: n_anglers #> Refused: refused #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Catch Data: 42 rows, 3 species #> caught: 8, harvested: 17, released: 17 #> Length Data: 20 rows, 3 species, length: 155–512 mm #> harvest: 14 individual #> release: 6 binned #> Sections: "none"

title: Unextrapolated Interview Summaries
text: Use summarize_refusals() understand many potential interviewees declined participate. High refusal rates can bias estimates refusers differ systematically participants. 22 interviews example_interviews accepted (refusals). field surveys, refusal rate 20% warrants investigation.
code: summarize_refusals(design) #>   month participation  N percent #> 1  June      accepted 22     100

title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
text: built creel_design object calendar, plot_design() shows number sampled days per stratum.  bar chart makes immediately clear whether weekday weekend strata balanced. tall weekday bar short weekend bar signals estimates weekend stratum carry higher uncertainty.
code: # Build a design from the bundled example calendar data("example_counts") cal <- unique(example_counts[, c("date", "day_type")]) design <- creel_design(cal, date = date, strata = day_type)  plot_design(design, title = "Sample sizes by stratum")

title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
text: counts attached, plot_design() switches jitter + crossbar display showing raw count distribution per stratum.  crossbar shows mean 95 % normal CI across sampled days. Large within-stratum spread (many jittered points far crossbar) suggests high day--day variability need sampling days.
code: design <- add_counts(design, example_counts)  plot_design(design, title = "Count distribution by stratum")

what: 2 Review the survey calendar with autoplot()
title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
text: autoplot.creel_schedule() renders monthly tile calendar creel_schedule object — object produced generate_schedule().  Blue tiles sampled weekdays; red tiles sampled weekends; grey tiles unsampled days. Month panels stack vertically full season visible glance.
code: # Generate a three-month schedule sampling 40 % of days schedule <- generate_schedule(   start_date    = "2024-06-01",   end_date      = "2024-08-31",   n_periods     = 2,   sampling_rate = 0.4,   seed          = 42 )  autoplot(schedule, title = "2024 Summer Creel Schedule")

title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
text: autoplot.creel_estimates() draws point--errorbar plot creel_estimates object.

title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
code: data("example_interviews")  design <- add_interviews(   design,   example_interviews,   catch       = catch_total,   effort      = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%)  effort <- estimate_effort(design)  autoplot(effort, title = "Total angler-effort estimate")

title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
text: Passing = day_type estimates effort separately stratum. resulting plot maps grouping variable x-axis point colour.  Weekend effort substantially higher weekday effort — typical pattern summer recreational fisheries.
code: effort_by_type <- estimate_effort(design, by = day_type)  autoplot(effort_by_type, title = "Angler-effort by day type")

title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
code: cpue <- estimate_catch_rate(design) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=17, 77.3% of 22 interviews) [default]  autoplot(cpue, title = "Walleye CPUE (catch per angler-hour)")

title: Visualising Creel Survey Designs and Results
text: three functions return standard ggplot objects, compose naturally + (ggplot2 operators) side--side using patchwork package installed.

title: tidycreel
text: Tidy Interface Creel Survey Design Analysis tidycreel provides pipe-friendly interface creel survey design, data management, estimation, reporting. Built survey package robust design-based inference, lets fisheries biologists work domain vocabulary — dates, strata, counts, effort — without managing survey design internals directly.

title: tidycreel
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("chrischizinski/tidycreel")  # or with devtools devtools::install_github("chrischizinski/tidycreel")

title: tidycreel
text: Stratified effort estimation periodic angler counts. PPS site selection Horvitz-Thompson estimators (Malvestuto 1978). Degenerate bus-route design certainty site sampling. Counter ingress-egress timestamp preprocessing. Single-overflight effort calibrated open-hours scaling.

title: tidycreel
text: Design-based inference — wraps survey package; biologists write creel vocabulary, survey package internals Single entry point — creel_design() dispatches correct estimator survey type Incomplete trip handling — TOST equivalence testing validates whether incomplete trips can pooled Sample size power — creel_n_effort(), creel_n_cpue(), creel_power() plan surveys data collection Season summaries — season_summary() assembles estimates across strata survey periods wide tibble

title: tidycreel
code: library(tidycreel)  # 1. Define survey structure with tidy selectors design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  # 2. Attach count observations design <- add_counts(design, example_counts)  # 3. Estimate effort with design-based variance estimate_effort(design)

title: tidycreel
code: # Probability-proportional-to-size site selection design <- creel_design(   example_calendar,   date = date,   strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route",   sampling_frame = my_site_frame,   site = site_id )  design <- add_interviews(design, interview_data,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   harvest = catch_kept )  estimate_catch_rate(design)

title: tidycreel
text: creel_design() — single entry point; dispatches survey_type (instantaneous, bus_route, ice, camera, aerial) add_counts(), add_interviews(), add_catch(), add_lengths(), add_sections() — attach observation data

title: tidycreel
text: estimate_effort() — total effort Taylor linearization, bootstrap, jackknife variance estimate_catch_rate() / estimate_harvest_rate() — ratio-based rates interview data estimate_total_catch() / estimate_total_harvest() — totals via delta method variance propagation

title: tidycreel
text: creel_n_effort(), creel_n_cpue(), creel_power() — sample size power analysis generate_schedule(), generate_bus_schedule() — sampling frame generators

title: tidycreel
text: validate_design(), check_completeness(), season_summary() — pre- post-season QA

title: Attach species-level catch data to a creel design — add_catch
