title: Attach species-level catch data to a creel design — add_catch
code: add_catch(design, data, catch_uid, interview_uid, species, count, catch_type)

title: Attach species-level catch data to a creel design — add_catch
text: design creel_design object created creel_design. data data frame long format: one row per species per catch type per interview. catch_uid <tidyselect> Column data containing interview IDs (catch-side join key). interview_uid <tidyselect> Column design$interviews containing matching interview IDs. species <tidyselect> Column data containing species names codes. count <tidyselect> Column data containing fish counts (non-negative integer numeric). catch_type <tidyselect> Column data containing catch fate: one "caught", "harvested", "released". Values normalized lowercase validation.

title: Attach species-level catch data to a creel design — add_catch
text: new creel_design object $catch associated $catch_*_col fields attached.

title: Attach species-level catch data to a creel design — add_catch
text: Catch type model: species-interview row carries one three catch types. "caught" total; "harvested" "released" subsets. "caught" row optional — absent, total catch inferred harvested + released. "caught" row present, caught >= harvested + released enforced (CATCH-04). Interview ID validation: Every interview ID appearing data must appear design$interviews[[interview_uid]]. Interviews catch rows valid (anglers caught nothing need appear catch data). Immutability: Returns new creel_design — input modified. Calling add_catch() design already $catch error.

title: Attach species-level catch data to a creel design — add_catch
code: data(example_calendar) data(example_interviews) data(example_catch)  design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- add_interviews(design, example_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished, harvest = catch_kept,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%) design <- add_catch(design, example_catch,   catch_uid = interview_id,   interview_uid = interview_id,   species = species,   count = count,   catch_type = catch_type ) print(design) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 14 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-14) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: "none" #> Interviews: 22 observations #> Type: "access" #> Catch: catch_total #> Effort: hours_fished #> Harvest: catch_kept #> Trip status: 17 complete, 5 incomplete #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Catch Data: 42 rows, 3 species #> caught: 8, harvested: 17, released: 17 #> Sections: "none"

what: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
text: Attaches instantaneous count data creel_design object constructs internal survey design object eagerly. core survey bridge layer - translating domain vocabulary (creel counts) statistical machinery (survey::svydesign). Eager construction catches design errors add_counts() time users context data adding.

title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
code: add_counts(   design,   counts,   psu = NULL,   count_time_col = NULL,   count_type = "instantaneous",   circuit_time = NULL,   period_length_col = NULL,   allow_invalid = FALSE )

title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
text: design creel_design object (created creel_design()) counts Data frame containing count data. Must : Date column matching design's date_col strata columns design's strata_cols least one numeric column (count variable) PSU column (specified via psu argument, defaults date_col) psu Character string naming PSU (Primary Sampling Unit) column count data. Defaults NULL, uses design's date_col PSU (day--PSU common creel design). designs, specify PSU column explicitly (e.g., "site_day" day-site PSUs). count_time_col Tidy selector column identifies distinct sub-PSU count observations (e.g., count_time_col = count_time count_time contains "" / "pm"). supplied, multiple rows per PSU aggregated single PSU-level mean (C-bar_d) within-day variance components (SS_d, K_d) stored design$within_day_var. NULL (default), single row per PSU expected; duplicate PSU rows emit CNT-06 warning. count_type Character string specifying count method. Must "instantaneous" (default) "progressive". "progressive", circuit_time period_length_col required. circuit_time Numeric. Circuit duration τ hours — time required complete one roving count circuit water body. Required count_type = "progressive" (CNT-05). Used compute κ = T_d / τ Ê_d = C × τ × κ. Ignored (warning) count_type = "instantaneous". period_length_col Tidy selector column containing T_d — total fishable shift duration hours PSU. Required count_type = "progressive" (CNT-05). Values must positive finite. column dropped design$counts Ê_d computed (must passed estimate_effort() count variable). allow_invalid Logical flag validation behavior. FALSE (default), validation failures abort detailed error messages. TRUE, validation failures generate warnings attach counts anyway (use caution).

title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
text: new creel_design object (list) components: calendar Original calendar data frame date_col Character name date column strata_cols Character vector strata column names site_col Character name site column, NULL design_type Character design type counts count data frame (newly attached) psu_col Character name PSU column survey Internal survey.design2 object (newly constructed) validation creel_validation object Tier 1 results

title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
text: add_counts() follows functional programming patterns returns new creel_design object. original design object modified. prevents accidental data loss makes workflow explicit: design2 <- add_counts(design, counts) add_counts(design, counts)

title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
text: add_counts() performs Tier 1 validation: Count data schema (Date column, numeric column) via validate_count_schema() Design column presence (date_col, strata_cols, PSU count data) NA values design-critical columns (date, strata, PSU) Survey construction (catches lonely PSU errors, stratification issues)

title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
text: Per user decision (clarified 2026-02-08), PSU specified add_counts(), creel_design() constructor. PSU meaningful count data present, making add_counts() correct abstraction boundary. design allows creel_design calendar used different PSU structures.

title: Attach count data to a creel design — add_counts
code: # Basic usage - day as PSU (default) calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  counts <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend"),   count = c(15, 23, 45, 52) )  design_with_counts <- add_counts(design, counts) #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability print(design_with_counts) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 4 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-04) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: 4 observations #> PSU column: date #> Count type: "instantaneous" #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Interviews: "none" #> Sections: "none"  # Custom PSU column counts_with_site_psu <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend"),   site_day = paste0("site_", 1:4),   count = c(15, 23, 45, 52) )  design2 <- add_counts(design, counts_with_site_psu, psu = "site_day") #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability  # Multiple counts per day (within-day variance via count_time_col) # Two circuits per day: "am" and "pm" calendar2 <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design3 <- creel_design(calendar2, date = date, strata = day_type)  multi_counts <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(rep(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04"), each = 2)),   day_type = rep(c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend"), each = 2),   count_time = rep(c("am", "pm"), 4),   n_anglers = c(12, 18, 20, 26, 40, 50, 48, 56) ) design_multi <- add_counts(design3, multi_counts,   count_time_col = count_time # nolint: object_usage_linter ) #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability  # Progressive count type (Ê_d = C x circuit_time x kappa) calendar3 <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design4 <- creel_design(calendar3, date = date, strata = day_type)  prog_counts <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend"),   n_anglers = c(15L, 23L, 45L, 52L),   shift_hours = rep(8, 4) ) design_prog <- add_counts(   design4, prog_counts,   count_type = "progressive",   circuit_time = 2,   period_length_col = shift_hours # nolint: object_usage_linter ) #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability

what: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
text: Attaches interview data (catch, effort, optionally harvest per completed fishing trip) creel_design object constructs internal interview survey design object eagerly. enables catch rate harvest rate estimation angler interviews. Follows tidy selector API pattern add_counts().

title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
code: add_interviews(   design,   interviews,   catch,   effort,   harvest = NULL,   trip_status,   trip_duration = NULL,   trip_start = NULL,   interview_time = NULL,   n_counted = NULL,   n_interviewed = NULL,   angler_type = NULL,   angler_method = NULL,   species_sought = NULL,   n_anglers = 1L,   refused = NULL,   date_col = NULL,   interview_type = c("access", "roving"),   allow_invalid = FALSE )

title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
text: design creel_design object (created creel_design()) interviews Data frame containing interview data. Must : Date column matching design's date_col Numeric catch column (total fish caught per trip) Numeric effort column (fishing time per trip, e.g., hours) Character trip status column ("complete" "incomplete") Optional numeric harvest column (fish kept per trip) Optional numeric trip duration column (hours) trip_start + interview_time columns (POSIXct) catch Tidy selector total catch column (required). Use bare column names (e.g., catch = catch_total) tidyselect helpers. effort Tidy selector fishing effort column (required, e.g., effort = hours_fished). represent time spent fishing per trip. harvest Tidy selector harvest (kept fish) column (optional, default NULL). provided, validated consistency (harvest <= catch). trip_status Tidy selector trip completion status column (required). Must contain "complete" "incomplete" (case-insensitive). essential downstream incomplete trip estimators. trip_duration Tidy selector trip duration column hours (optional, default NULL). Provide either trip_duration trip_start + interview_time, . Duration values must positive >= 1/60 hours (1 minute). trip_start Tidy selector trip start time column (optional, default NULL). Must POSIXct POSIXlt. Requires interview_time calculate duration. Use duration needs calculated timestamps. interview_time Tidy selector interview time column (optional, default NULL). Must POSIXct POSIXlt. Requires trip_start calculate duration. Duration calculated interview_time - trip_start hours. n_counted Tidy selector count anglers observed site sampling period (required bus-route designs, ignored designs). Values must non-negative integers. Must satisfy n_counted >= n_interviewed. n_interviewed Tidy selector count anglers actually interviewed site (required bus-route designs, ignored designs). Values 0 valid (anglers came water). angler_type Tidy selector angler type column (optional, default NULL). Use bare column names (e.g., angler_type = angler_type). Common values "bank" "boat". validated Phase 28; downstream summary functions use field. angler_method Tidy selector angler method column (optional, default NULL). Use bare column names (e.g., angler_method = method_code). Records fishing technique employed (e.g., "fly", "spin", "bait"). species_sought Tidy selector species sought column (optional, default NULL). Use bare column names (e.g., species_sought = target_species). Records species angler targeting interview. n_anglers Tidy selector number anglers party (default 1L – individual-level interviews). omitted, cli_inform() message notes assumption. Use bare column names (e.g., n_anglers = party_size). refused Tidy selector refused interview flag column (optional, default NULL). Use bare column names (e.g., refused = refused_flag). Values logical (TRUE/FALSE) coercible logical. date_col Character name date column interviews (default NULL, uses design's date_col). Specify explicitly interview data uses different date column name design calendar. interview_type Character: "access" (complete trips access point) "roving" (incomplete trips fishing). Default "access". affects catch rates calculated estimation functions. allow_invalid Logical flag validation behavior. FALSE (default), validation failures abort detailed error messages. TRUE, validation failures generate warnings attach interviews anyway (use caution).

title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
text: new creel_design object (list) components: calendar Original calendar data frame date_col Character name date column strata_cols Character vector strata column names site_col Character name site column, NULL design_type Character design type counts Count data frame (previously attached, NULL) interviews interview data frame (newly attached) catch_col Character name catch column effort_col Character name effort column harvest_col Character name harvest column, NULL trip_status_col Character name trip status column trip_duration_col Character name trip duration column, NULL trip_start_col Character name trip start time column, NULL interview_time_col Character name interview time column, NULL interview_type Character interview type interview_survey Internal survey.design2 object (newly constructed) validation creel_validation object Tier 1 results

title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
text: add_interviews() follows functional programming patterns returns new creel_design object. original design object modified. prevents accidental data loss makes workflow explicit: design2 <- add_interviews(design, interviews, ...) add_interviews(design, interviews, ...)

title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
text: add_interviews() performs Tier 1 validation: Interview data schema (Date column, numeric columns) via validate_interview_schema() Design column presence (date_col exists interview data) NA values date column Interview dates exist design calendar Catch effort columns exist numeric Harvest column exists numeric (provided) Harvest <= catch consistency (harvest provided) Trip status valid ("complete" "incomplete", case-insensitive) Trip status NA values Trip duration/time inputs mutually exclusive (error provided) Trip duration positive, >= 1 minute, warns > 48 hours Trip start + interview_time POSIXct/POSIXlt, calculate valid duration Interview survey construction (catches stratification issues)

title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
text: Interview dates automatically linked design calendar via date matching. Strata calendar inherited interview data, enabling stratified estimation catch rates.

title: Attach interview data to a creel design — add_interviews
code: # Basic usage - with trip status and duration calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   catch_total = c(5, 3, 7, 2),   hours_fished = c(2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 1.5),   trip_status = c("complete", "complete", "incomplete", "complete"),   trip_duration = c(2.0, 2.5, 1.5, 1.5) )  design_with_interviews <- add_interviews(   design, interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 interviews: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 interviews #> • Stratum weekend: 2 interviews #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> ℹ Added 4 interviews: 3 complete (75%), 1 incomplete (25%) print(design_with_interviews) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 4 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-04) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: "none" #> Interviews: 4 observations #> Type: "access" #> Catch: catch_total #> Effort: hours_fished #> Trip status: 3 complete, 1 incomplete #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Sections: "none"  # With harvest column and calculated duration from timestamps interviews2 <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   catch_total = c(5, 3, 7, 2),   catch_kept = c(2, 1, 5, 2),   hours_fished = c(2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 1.5),   trip_status = c("complete", "incomplete", "complete", "complete"),   trip_start = as.POSIXct(c(     "2024-06-01 08:00", "2024-06-02 09:00",     "2024-06-03 07:00", "2024-06-04 10:00"   )),   interview_time = as.POSIXct(c(     "2024-06-01 10:00", "2024-06-02 11:30",     "2024-06-03 10:00", "2024-06-04 11:30"   )) )  design2 <- add_interviews(   design, interviews2,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   harvest = catch_kept,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_start = trip_start,   interview_time = interview_time ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 interviews: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 interviews #> • Stratum weekend: 2 interviews #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> ℹ Added 4 interviews: 3 complete (75%), 1 incomplete (25%)

what: Attach fish length frequency data to a creel design — add_lengths
title: Attach fish length frequency data to a creel design — add_lengths
text: Attaches long-format data frame fish length measurements creel_design object. Supports individual measurements (harvest) binned counts (release). Data validated attach time stored design use downstream summary estimation functions.

title: Attach fish length frequency data to a creel design — add_lengths
code: add_lengths(   design,   data,   length_uid,   interview_uid,   species,   length,   length_type,   count = NULL,   release_format = "individual" )

title: Attach fish length frequency data to a creel design — add_lengths
text: design creel_design object created creel_design. data data frame long format: one row per fish measurement length bin per species per interview. length_uid <tidyselect> Column data containing interview IDs (length-side join key). interview_uid <tidyselect> Column design$interviews containing matching interview IDs. species <tidyselect> Column data containing species names codes. length <tidyselect> Column data containing length values. harvest rows (release_format = "individual"), must numeric (mm). release rows release_format = "binned", may character bin label "300-350". length_type <tidyselect> Column data containing measurement fate: one "harvest" "release". Values normalized lowercase validation. count <tidyselect> Optional. Column data containing fish counts binned release rows. Required release_format = "binned" release rows present. Harvest rows NA column. Omit (pass NULL) length data individual measurements. release_format Character scalar: "individual" (default) "binned". Controls release rows validated length range computed display.

title: Attach fish length frequency data to a creel design — add_lengths
text: new creel_design object $lengths associated $lengths_*_col fields attached.

title: Attach fish length frequency data to a creel design — add_lengths
text: Mixed column type footgun: length column may contain numeric values (harvest rows) character bin labels (release rows). R coerce entire column character mixing types data.frame. add_lengths() validates harvest row lengths subsetting harvest rows first, attempting .numeric() coercion, avoid errors mixed-type column. Interview ID validation: Every interview ID appearing data must appear design$interviews[[interview_uid]]. Interviews length rows valid. Immutability: Returns new creel_design — input modified. Calling add_lengths() design already $lengths error.

title: Attach fish length frequency data to a creel design — add_lengths
code: data(example_calendar) data(example_interviews) data(example_lengths)  design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- add_interviews(design, example_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished, harvest = catch_kept,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%) design <- add_lengths(design, example_lengths,   length_uid = interview_id,   interview_uid = interview_id,   species = species,   length = length,   length_type = length_type,   count = count,   release_format = "binned" ) print(design) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Design ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: "instantaneous" #> Date column: date #> Strata: day_type #> Calendar: 14 days (2024-06-01 to 2024-06-14) #> day_type: 2 levels #> Counts: "none" #> Interviews: 22 observations #> Type: "access" #> Catch: catch_total #> Effort: hours_fished #> Harvest: catch_kept #> Trip status: 17 complete, 5 incomplete #> Survey: <survey.design2> (constructed) #> Length Data: 20 rows, 3 species, length: 155–512 mm #> harvest: 14 individual #> release: 6 binned #> Sections: "none"

what: Register spatial sections for a creel survey design — add_sections
title: Register spatial sections for a creel survey design — add_sections
text: Attaches sections registry creel_design object. sections registered, subsequent calls add_counts() add_interviews() validate every row's section value matches registered section name. Unrecognised section values abort informative error identifying bad values listing valid options. add_sections() optional single-section surveys. Call survey covers multiple named sections want early detection mislabelled data (e.g. "NRTH" instead "NORTH").

title: Register spatial sections for a creel survey design — add_sections
code: add_sections(   design,   sections,   section_col,   description_col = NULL,   area_col = NULL,   shoreline_col = NULL )

title: Register spatial sections for a creel survey design — add_sections
text: design creel_design object (created creel_design()). sections data frame one row per section. Must contain column identified section_col. Optional metadata columns identified description_col, area_col, shoreline_col. section_col Tidy selector column sections holds section names IDs. Must character factor. duplicate values permitted. description_col Optional tidy selector free-text description column (e.g. "North inlet", "Main basin"). Stored reporting . area_col Optional tidy selector surface area column (numeric, ha). values must strictly positive. Stored now; used v0.8.0 aerial survey estimation. shoreline_col Optional tidy selector shoreline length column (numeric, km). values must strictly positive. Stored now; used v0.8.0 aerial survey estimation.

title: Register spatial sections for a creel survey design — add_sections
text: new creel_design object $sections $section_col populated. input design modified.

title: Register spatial sections for a creel survey design — add_sections
text: add_sections() called, add_counts() add_interviews() check every row's section value present design$sections[[design$section_col]]. unrecognised value produces cli_abort() naming bad values listing valid section names.

title: Register spatial sections for a creel survey design — add_sections
code: cal <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c(     "2024-06-01", "2024-06-02",     "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04"   )),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(cal, date = date, strata = day_type)  my_sections <- data.frame(   section      = c("North Inlet", "Main Basin", "South Outlet"),   description  = c("Tributary inlet", "Open water", "Dam outlet"),   area_ha      = c(45.0, 820.0, 12.0),   shoreline_km = c(8.2, 62.1, 3.4) )  design2 <- add_sections(design, my_sections,   section_col     = section,   description_col = description,   area_col        = area_ha,   shoreline_col   = shoreline_km )

what: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
title: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
text: Applies nonresponse weighting creel_design object scaling survey weights within stratum inverse observed response rate. adjustment uses postStratify (default) calibrate update internal svydesign object, downstream estimators (estimate_effort, estimate_catch_rate, etc.) automatically use corrected weights.

title: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
code: adjust_nonresponse(   design,   response_rates,   method = c("postStratify", "calibrate"),   stratum_col = "stratum" )

title: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
text: design creel_design object least one survey sub-object already attached (add_counts add_interviews). response_rates data frame tibble one row per stratum, containing minimum columns: stratum Character factor. Stratum identifier matching values design's strata column. n_sampled Integer. Number units approached interview stratum. n_responded Integer. Number units actually responded. Must <= n_sampled. method Character. Weighting method apply. Either "postStratify" (default, adjusts weights match known stratum totals inversely proportional response rate) "calibrate" (calibration estimator via survey::calibrate; requires stratum population totals response_rates). stratum_col Character. Name stratum column response_rates (default: "stratum").

title: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
text: input creel_design adjusted weights. updated design includes attribute "nonresponse_diagnostics" (tibble columns stratum, n_sampled, n_responded, response_rate, weight_adjustment) can retrieved attr(result, "nonresponse_diagnostics").

title: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
text: stratum \(h\): $$response\_rate_h = n\_responded_h / n\_sampled_h$$ $$weight\_adjustment_h = 1 / response\_rate_h$$ original weights multiplied weight_adjustment_h, upweights respondents represent non-respondents (Armstrong & Overton 1977). assumes respondents non-respondents exchangeable within strata (missing--random assumption). implausible, sensitivity analysis comparing pre- post-adjustment estimates recommended.

title: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
text: Armstrong, B.G. Overton, W.S. 1977. Estimating nonresponse bias mail surveys. Journal Marketing Research 14:396–402. Pollock, K.H., Jones, C.M. Brown, T.L. 1994. Angler Survey Methods Applications Fisheries Management. American Fisheries Society, Bethesda, MD.

title: Adjust a creel design for nonresponse bias — adjust_nonresponse
code: data("example_counts", package = "tidycreel") data("example_interviews", package = "tidycreel") cal     <- unique(example_counts[, c("date", "day_type")]) design  <- creel_design(cal, date = date, strata = day_type) design  <- suppressWarnings(add_counts(design, example_counts)) design  <- suppressWarnings(add_interviews(   design, example_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration )) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%)  resp <- data.frame(   stratum     = c("weekday", "weekend"),   n_sampled   = c(80L, 60L),   n_responded = c(72L, 48L) ) adj_design <- adjust_nonresponse(design, resp) attr(adj_design, "nonresponse_diagnostics") #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   stratum n_sampled n_responded response_rate weight_adjustment #>   <chr>       <int>       <int>         <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 weekday        80          72           0.9              1.11 #> 2 weekend        60          48           0.8              1.25

what: Extract internal survey design object for advanced use — as_survey_design
title: Extract internal survey design object for advanced use — as_survey_design

title: Extract internal survey design object for advanced use — as_survey_design

title: Extract internal survey design object for advanced use — as_survey_design

title: Extract internal survey design object for advanced use — as_survey_design
code: # Basic workflow library(survey) #> Loading required package: grid #> Loading required package: Matrix #> Loading required package: survival #>  #> Attaching package: ‘survey’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:graphics’: #>  #>     dotchart cal <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(cal, date = date, strata = day_type)  counts <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend"),   count = c(15, 23, 45, 52) )  design2 <- add_counts(design, counts) #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability  # Extract survey object for advanced use svy <- as_survey_design(design2) #> Warning: Accessing internal survey design object. #> ℹ This is an advanced feature. Most users should use {.fn estimate_effort} instead. #> ! Modifying the survey design may produce incorrect variance estimates. #> This warning is displayed once per session.  # Use with survey package functions survey::svytotal(~count, svy) #>       total    SE #> count   135 10.63 survey::svymean(~count, svy) #>        mean     SE #> count 33.75 2.6575

what: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
title: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
text: Takes creel_estimates object produced variance = "taylor" re-estimates using replicate weights (bootstrap jackknife) produce side--side comparison standard errors. cli_warn() issued row two SEs diverge divergence_threshold.

title: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
code: compare_variance(   x,   replicate_method = c("bootstrap", "jackknife"),   conf_level = 0.95,   divergence_threshold = 0.1,   ... )

title: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
text: x creel_estimates object variance_method =   "taylor". Must created design stored x$design. replicate_method Character. Replicate variance method use comparison. One "bootstrap" (default) "jackknife". conf_level Numeric confidence level (default: 0.95). Passed replicate estimation call. divergence_threshold Numeric. Fraction replicate SE may differ Taylor SE warning issued (default: 0.10 = 10\ warning fires group |se_replicate / se_taylor - 1| > divergence_threshold. ... Additional arguments passed underlying estimator.

title: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
text: creel_variance_comparison S3 object (tibble subclass) columns: se_taylor Taylor linearization SE original estimate. se_replicate Replicate-weight SE re-estimation. divergence_ratio Ratio se_replicate / se_taylor. NA se_taylor == 0. diverges_flag Logical. TRUE |divergence_ratio - 1| > divergence_threshold. Group columns () preserved. full tibble returned invisibly via print(). Use .data.frame() standard tibble methods processing.

title: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
text: function extracts Taylor SE x$estimates$se, calls estimator produced x (resolved via x$method) variance = replicate_method. re-estimation uses x$design grouping variables x$by_vars. Divergence computed : $$ratio = se_{replicate} / se_{taylor}$$ $$diverges = |ratio - 1| > threshold$$ ratio substantially different 1 indicates Taylor approximation may unreliable design (e.g., sparse strata, non-linear estimator). Replication-based variance generally robust slower compute.

title: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
text: Wolter, K.M. 2007. Introduction Variance Estimation, 2nd ed. Springer. Lumley, T. 2010. Complex Surveys: Guide Analysis Using R. Wiley.

title: Compare Taylor linearization vs. replicate variance for creel estimates — compare_variance
code: data("example_counts", package = "tidycreel") data("example_interviews", package = "tidycreel") cal <- unique(example_counts[, c("date", "day_type")]) design <- creel_design(cal, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- suppressWarnings(add_counts(design, example_counts)) design <- suppressWarnings(add_interviews(   design, example_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration )) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%) taylor_est <- suppressWarnings(estimate_catch_rate(design)) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=17, 77.3% of 22 interviews) [default] cmp <- suppressWarnings(compare_variance(taylor_est)) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=17, 100% of 17 interviews) [default] print(cmp) #>  #> ── Variance Comparison: Taylor vs. bootstrap ─────────────────────────────────── #> Divergence threshold: 10% #> ✔ All rows within threshold. #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   se_taylor se_replicate divergence_ratio diverges_flag #>       <dbl>        <dbl>            <dbl> <lgl>         #> 1     0.114        0.115             1.01 FALSE

what: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: Constructs creel_design object calendar data tidy column selection. entry point creel survey analysis workflows. design object stores survey structure (date, strata, optional site), validates input data (Tier 1 validation), serves foundation adding count data estimating effort. bus-route surveys nonuniform site selection probabilities, use survey_type = "bus_route" supply sampling_frame data frame specifying sites, circuits, sampling probabilities.

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
code: creel_design(   calendar,   date,   strata,   site = NULL,   design_type = "instantaneous",   survey_type = design_type,   sampling_frame = NULL,   p_site = NULL,   p_period = NULL,   circuit = NULL,   effort_type = NULL,   camera_mode = NULL,   h_open = NULL,   visibility_correction = NULL )

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: calendar data frame containing calendar data date strata columns. Must least one Date column one character/factor column (validated via internal schema check). date Tidy selector date column. Must select exactly one column class Date. Accepts bare column names tidyselect helpers (e.g., starts_with("date")). strata Tidy selector strata columns. Can select one columns class character factor. Accepts bare column names tidyselect helpers (e.g., c(day_type, season) starts_with("day")). site Optional tidy selector site column. instantaneous designs, selects calendar. bus-route designs (survey_type = "bus_route"), selects site ID column sampling_frame. Must select exactly one column class character factor. Default NULL (single-site survey instantaneous designs; required bus-route designs). design_type Character string specifying survey design type. Default "instantaneous". Kept backward compatibility; use survey_type new code. survey_type Character string specifying survey type. Default inherits design_type ("instantaneous"). Use "bus_route" nonuniform probability bus-route surveys (BUSRT-06, BUSRT-07). survey_type design_type refer concept; survey_type canonical parameter new designs. sampling_frame Data frame site, circuit, probability columns. Required survey_type = "bus_route". row represents one site-circuit sampling unit inclusion probability components (p_site p_period). p_site Tidy selector site sampling probability column sampling_frame. Required survey_type = "bus_route". Values must (0, 1] must sum 1.0 within circuit (tolerance 1e-6). p_period Tidy selector period sampling probability column sampling_frame, scalar numeric value (0, 1] applies globally rows. Required survey_type = "bus_route". circuit Optional tidy selector circuit ID column sampling_frame. circuit route x period combination. omitted, rows treated belonging single unnamed circuit (".default"). Required multi-circuit designs. effort_type Character string specifying type effort measured ice fishing surveys. Required survey_type = "ice". Must one "time_on_ice" (total hours angler ice) "active_fishing_time" (hours actively fishing, excluding travel/setup). value controls column name estimate_effort() output: total_effort_hr_on_ice total_effort_hr_active. camera_mode Character string specifying camera sub-mode. Required survey_type = "camera". Must one "counter" (camera records daily ingress total) "ingress_egress" (camera records individual arrival/departure timestamps, preprocessed preprocess_camera_timestamps() calling add_counts()). h_open Positive numeric scalar specifying number hours fishery open per day. Required survey_type = "aerial". Used expansion factor aerial effort estimator: \(\hat{E} = N_{obs} \times h_{open} / v\). visibility_correction Optional numeric scalar (0, 1] specifying proportion anglers water detectable aircraft. Used survey_type = "aerial". Defaults 1.0 (anglers visible) NULL. value 0.85 means 85% anglers detected; effort estimate scaled \(1 / 0.85\).

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: creel_design S3 object (list) components: calendar original calendar data frame date_col Character name date column strata_cols Character vector strata column names site_col Character name site column, NULL design_type Character design type counts NULL (populated add_counts() future) survey NULL (populated internally estimation) bus_route List resolved sampling frame data column mappings, NULL non-bus-route designs. Contains: $data (sampling frame .pi_i column added), $site_col, $circuit_col, $p_site_col, $p_period_col, $pi_i_col (always ".pi_i").

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: constructor performs fail-fast validation: Date column class Date (character, numeric, POSIXct) Date column contains NA values Strata columns character factor (numeric, logical) Site column (provided) character factor (bus_route ) p_site p_period values (0, 1] (bus_route ) p_site values sum 1.0 within circuit (tolerance 1e-6) (bus_route ) p_period values constant within circuit (tolerance 1e-10)

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
text: Jones, C. M., & Pollock, K. H. (2012). Recreational survey methods: estimating effort, harvest, abundance. . V. Zale, D. L. Parrish, & T. M. Sutton (Eds.), Fisheries Techniques (3rd ed., pp. 883–919). American Fisheries Society. Eq. 19.4 19.5 define bus-route estimators; pp. 883–884 define inclusion probability \(\pi_i = p_{\text{site}} \times p_{\text{period}}\).

title: Create a creel survey design — creel_design
code: # Basic design with single stratum calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  # Multiple strata calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   season = c("summer", "summer") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = c(day_type, season))  # With site column for multi-site survey calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   lake = c("lake_a", "lake_b") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type, site = lake)  # Using tidyselect helpers calendar <- data.frame(   survey_date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   day_period = c("morning", "evening") ) design <- creel_design(   calendar,   date = starts_with("survey"),   strata = starts_with("day") )  # Bus-route design with scalar p_period calendar_br <- data.frame(   date = as.Date("2024-06-01"),   day_type = "weekday" ) sf <- data.frame(   site = c("A", "B", "C"),   p_site = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),   p_period = 0.5 ) design_br <- creel_design(   calendar_br,   date = date,   strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route",   sampling_frame = sf,   site = site,   p_site = p_site,   p_period = p_period )

what: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: Determines number interviews needed achieve target coefficient variation CPUE (catch-per-unit-effort) ratio estimate, using ratio-estimator variance approximation Cochran (1977).

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
code: creel_n_cpue(cv_catch, cv_effort, rho = 0, cv_target)

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: cv_catch Numeric scalar. Pilot coefficient variation catch per interview (numerator CPUE ratio). Must > 0. cv_effort Numeric scalar. Pilot coefficient variation effort per interview (denominator CPUE ratio). Must > 0. rho Numeric scalar. Pilot correlation catch effort per interview. Must [-1, 1]. Default 0 (conservative; -estimates required n catch effort positively correlated). cv_target Numeric scalar. Target coefficient variation CPUE estimate. Must (0, 1].

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: integer scalar (>= 1): number interviews required.

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: Implements ratio-estimator variance approximation Cochran (1977, Chapter 6) parameterised terms coefficients variation rather raw variances, natural pre-season planning: $$n = \left\lceil \frac{CV_{catch}^2 + CV_{effort}^2   - 2 \rho \cdot CV_{catch} \cdot CV_{effort}}{CV_{target}^2} \right\rceil$$ Setting rho = 0 (default) conservative: -estimates required sample size catch effort positively correlated. Users pilot data supply observed correlation obtain less conservative estimate. result floored 1L ensure least one interview recommended.

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
text: Cochran, W.G. 1977. Sampling Techniques, 3rd ed. Wiley, New York. Chapter 6 (ratio estimator variance approximation).

title: Calculate interviews required to achieve a target CV on CPUE — creel_n_cpue
code: # rho = 0 (conservative, no pilot correlation data) creel_n_cpue(cv_catch = 0.8, cv_effort = 0.5, rho = 0, cv_target = 0.20) #> [1] 23  # With known positive correlation (smaller n) creel_n_cpue(cv_catch = 0.8, cv_effort = 0.5, rho = 0.5, cv_target = 0.20) #> [1] 13

what: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: Uses stratified sample size formula McCormick & Quist (2017) determine many sampling days needed achieve target coefficient variation effort estimate, given pilot variance estimates per day-type stratum.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
code: creel_n_effort(cv_target, N_h, ybar_h, s2_h)

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: cv_target Numeric scalar. Target coefficient variation effort estimate (e.g., 0.20 20 percent). Must (0, 1]. N_h Named numeric vector. Total available days per stratum (e.g., c(weekday = 65, weekend = 28)). Values must >= 1. ybar_h Numeric vector length N_h. Pilot mean effort per day per stratum (e.g., angler-hours per day). Values must >= 0. s2_h Numeric vector length N_h. Pilot variance effort per day per stratum. Values must >= 0.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: named integer vector. Elements named strata N_h give sampling days required per stratum; element "total" gives overall sample size proportional allocation.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: Implements Cochran (1977) equation 5.25 proportional allocation, applied creel surveys McCormick & Quist (2017). finite-population correction (FPC) factor intentionally omitted (standard practice pre-season planning goal determine many days sample, assess precision completed survey). per-stratum sample sizes n_h computed total n_total proportional allocation: n_h = ceiling(n_total * N_h / sum(N_h)). stratum ceiling-ed independently, sum(n_h) may exceed n_total.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
text: McCormick, J.L. Quist, M.C. 2017. Sample size estimation -site creel surveys. North American Journal Fisheries Management 37:970-983. doi:10.1080/02755947.2017.1342723 Cochran, W.G. 1977. Sampling Techniques, 3rd ed. Wiley, New York.

title: Calculate sampling days required to achieve a target CV on effort — creel_n_effort
code: # Two-stratum weekday/weekend example creel_n_effort(   cv_target = 0.20,   N_h = c(weekday = 65, weekend = 28),   ybar_h = c(50, 60),   s2_h = c(400, 500) ) #> weekday weekend   total  #>       3       2       4

what: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: Calculates probability detecting fractional change CPUE given target sample size per season, historical CV, significance level. Uses two-sample normal approximation equal group sizes.

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
code: creel_power(   n,   cv_historical,   delta_pct,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "one.sided") )

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: n Integerish scalar (>= 1). Number interviews per season. cv_historical Numeric scalar (> 0). Coefficient variation CPUE historical pilot data. delta_pct Numeric scalar (> 0). Fractional change detect, expressed proportion — e.g., 0.20 20 percent change. Note: fraction, percentage point. alpha Numeric scalar (0, 0.5]. Type error rate. Default 0.05. alternative Character. Either "two.sided" (default) "one.sided".

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: numeric scalar (0, 1): estimated statistical power.

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: Implements two-sample normal approximation power equal group sizes, parameterised terms CV: $$ncp = |\delta| \cdot \sqrt{n/2} \, / \, CV_{historical}$$ alternative = "two.sided": $$power = \Phi(ncp - z_{\alpha/2}) + \Phi(-ncp - z_{\alpha/2})$$ alternative = "one.sided": $$power = \Phi(ncp - z_{\alpha})$$ delta fractional effect size (delta_pct), n number interviews per season, CV_historical pilot CV CPUE. warning issued delta_pct > 5 values greater 5 almost certainly input percentage-point form rather fractional form (e.g., 20 instead 0.20).

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
text: Cohen, J. 1988. Statistical Power Analysis Behavioral Sciences, 2nd ed. Lawrence Erlbaum Associates, Hillsdale, NJ.

title: Estimate statistical power to detect a change in CPUE between seasons — creel_power
code: # Two-sided power at n = 100, CV = 0.5, 20 percent change creel_power(n = 100, cv_historical = 0.5, delta_pct = 0.20) #> [1] 0.8074304  # One-sided test (higher power for same inputs) creel_power(n = 100, cv_historical = 0.5, delta_pct = 0.20, alternative = "one.sided") #> [1] 0.881709

what: Column-mapping contract for tidycreel data sources — creel_schema

text: creel_schema() constructs creel_schema S3 object maps canonical tidycreel column names actual column table names data source. schema full connection contract consumed creel_connect() fetch_*() functions tidycreel.connect companion package. Construction permissive — column arguments default NULL. Use validate_creel_schema() check required columns given survey type mapped.

title: Column-mapping contract for tidycreel data sources — creel_schema
code: creel_schema(   survey_type = c("instantaneous", "bus_route", "ice", "camera", "aerial"),   interviews_table = NULL,   counts_table = NULL,   catch_table = NULL,   lengths_table = NULL,   date_col = NULL,   catch_col = NULL,   effort_col = NULL,   trip_status_col = NULL,   count_col = NULL,   catch_uid_col = NULL,   interview_uid_col = NULL,   species_col = NULL,   catch_count_col = NULL,   catch_type_col = NULL,   length_uid_col = NULL,   length_mm_col = NULL,   length_type_col = NULL,   harvest_col = NULL,   trip_duration_col = NULL,   trip_start_col = NULL,   interview_time_col = NULL,   n_anglers_col = NULL,   n_counted_col = NULL,   n_interviewed_col = NULL,   angler_type_col = NULL,   angler_method_col = NULL,   species_sought_col = NULL,   refused_col = NULL )

title: Column-mapping contract for tidycreel data sources — creel_schema
text: survey_type Survey type. One "instantaneous", "bus_route", "ice", "camera", "aerial". Validated construction via match.arg(). interviews_table Name interviews table data source. counts_table Name counts table data source. catch_table Name catch table data source. lengths_table Name lengths table data source. date_col Column name survey date. catch_col Column name catch count interviews. effort_col Column name effort (hours) interviews. trip_status_col Column name trip status interviews. count_col Column name angler count counts. catch_uid_col Column name catch unique identifier. interview_uid_col Column name interview unique identifier. species_col Column name species. catch_count_col Column name catch count catch table. catch_type_col Column name catch type (harvest/release). length_uid_col Column name length unique identifier. length_mm_col Column name fish length (mm). length_type_col Column name length type. harvest_col Column name harvest count. trip_duration_col Column name trip duration. trip_start_col Column name trip start time. interview_time_col Column name interview time. n_anglers_col Column name number anglers. n_counted_col Column name number anglers counted. n_interviewed_col Column name number anglers interviewed. angler_type_col Column name angler type. angler_method_col Column name fishing method. species_sought_col Column name target species. refused_col Column name refused interviews indicator.

title: Column-mapping contract for tidycreel data sources — creel_schema
text: creel_schema S3 object.

title: Column-mapping contract for tidycreel data sources — creel_schema
code: s <- creel_schema(   survey_type      = "instantaneous",   interviews_table = "vwInterviews",   counts_table     = "vwCounts",   date_col         = "SurveyDate",   catch_col        = "TotalCatch",   effort_col       = "EffortHours",   trip_status_col  = "TripStatus",   count_col        = "AnglerCount" ) print(s) #> <creel_schema: instantaneous> #>  #> ── interviews: vwInterviews ── #>  #> date -> SurveyDate #> catch -> TotalCatch #> effort -> EffortHours #> trip_status -> TripStatus #>  #> ── counts: vwCounts ── #>  #> count -> AnglerCount

what: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: Calculates coefficient variation attainable given fixed sample size, acting algebraic inverse creel_n_effort() (type = "effort") creel_n_cpue() (type = "cpue").

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
code: cv_from_n(type = c("effort", "cpue"), n, ...)

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: type Character. Either "effort" "cpue". Selects formula branch required additional arguments. n Integerish scalar (>= 1). Available sample size (sampling days "effort", interviews "cpue"). ... Additional arguments passed relevant branch: type = "effort": N_h — Named numeric vector; total available days per stratum (>= 1). ybar_h — Numeric vector; pilot mean effort per day per stratum (>= 0). s2_h — Numeric vector; pilot variance effort per day per stratum (>= 0). type = "cpue": cv_catch — Numeric scalar; pilot CV catch per interview (> 0). cv_effort — Numeric scalar; pilot CV effort per interview (> 0). rho — Numeric scalar; pilot correlation catch effort, [-1, 1]. Default 0.

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: numeric scalar (> 0): expected CV achievable sample size n.

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: Effort branch (type = "effort"): $$CV = \frac{\sqrt{\sum_h N_h s_h^2 / n}}{\sum_h N_h \bar{y}_h}$$ inverse Cochran (1977) stratified sample-size formula implemented creel_n_effort(). CPUE branch (type = "cpue"): $$CV = \sqrt{(CV_{catch}^2 + CV_{effort}^2   - 2\rho \cdot CV_{catch} \cdot CV_{effort}) / n}$$ inverse ratio-estimator formula implemented creel_n_cpue(). creel_n_effort() creel_n_cpue() apply ceiling(), round-trip property cv_from_n(type, n = creel_n_*(cv, ...), ...) <= cv (recovered CV target).

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
text: Cochran, W.G. 1977. Sampling Techniques, 3rd ed. Wiley, New York.

title: Compute the expected CV achievable with a known sample size — cv_from_n
code: # Effort round-trip n_days <- creel_n_effort(0.20,   N_h = c(weekday = 65, weekend = 28),   ybar_h = c(50, 60), s2_h = c(400, 500) ) cv_from_n("effort",   n = n_days[["total"]],   N_h = c(weekday = 65, weekend = 28),   ybar_h = c(50, 60), s2_h = c(400, 500) ) #> [1] 0.02028398  # CPUE round-trip n_int <- creel_n_cpue(cv_catch = 0.8, cv_effort = 0.5, rho = 0, cv_target = 0.20) cv_from_n("cpue", n = n_int, cv_catch = 0.8, cv_effort = 0.5, rho = 0) #> [1] 0.1967121

what: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
text: Computes CPUE estimates standard errors confidence intervals creel survey design attached interview data. Supports ratio--means (complete trips) mean--ratios (incomplete trips) estimation methods.

title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
code: estimate_catch_rate(   design,   by = NULL,   variance = "taylor",   conf_level = 0.95,   estimator = "ratio-of-means",   use_trips = NULL,   truncate_at = 0.5,   targeted = TRUE,   missing_sections = "warn" )

title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
text: design creel_design object interviews attached via add_interviews. design must interview survey object constructed catch effort columns. Optional tidy selector grouping variables. Accepts bare column names (e.g., = day_type), multiple columns (e.g., = c(day_type, location)), tidyselect helpers (e.g., = starts_with("day")). NULL (default), computes single CPUE estimate across interviews. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method. Options: "taylor" (default, Taylor linearization), "bootstrap" (bootstrap resampling 500 replicates), "jackknife" (jackknife resampling, automatic JKn/JK1 selection). conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals (default: 0.95 95% confidence intervals). Must 0 1. estimator Character string specifying estimation method. Options: "ratio--means" (default, complete trips), "mor" (mean--ratios, incomplete trips), "mortr" (truncated mean--ratios — "mor" truncate_at mandatory defaults 0.5 h). MOR MORtr require trip_status field error incomplete trips available. See Details. use_trips Character string specifying trip type use trip_status field provided. Options: "complete" (default NULL) uses complete trips ratio--means estimator, "incomplete" uses incomplete trips mean--ratios estimator, "diagnostic" estimates CPUE using trip types returns comparison table. Following Pollock et al. (1994), complete trips scientifically preferred (length--stay bias). Incomplete trip estimation diagnostic/research mode requiring validation. Diagnostic mode requires complete incomplete trips present. Default NULL defaults "complete". Parameter ignored trip_status field provided (perfect backward compatibility). See Details. truncate_at Numeric minimum trip duration (hours) MOR estimation. Default 0.5 hours (30 minutes) per Hoenig et al. (1997) prevent unstable variance short trips. Trips duration < truncate_at excluded MOR estimation. Set NULL disable truncation (research mode ). Ignored ratio--means estimator. targeted Logical. TRUE (default), trips used. FALSE, zero-effort trips excluded MOR/MORtr estimation — appropriate non-targeted species trips zero catch. cli_warn() emitted 70\ zero catch targeted = TRUE (possible mis-specification). Ignored ratio--means estimator. missing_sections Character string controlling behavior registered section interview observations. "warn" (default) emits cli_warn() inserts NA row data_available = FALSE. "error" aborts cli_abort(). Ignored non-sectioned designs.

title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
text: creel_estimates S3 object (list) components: estimates (tibble estimate, se, ci_lower, ci_upper, n columns, plus grouping columns specified), method (character: "ratio--means-cpue" "mean--ratios-cpue", "-per-angler" suffix normalized), variance_method (character: reflects variance parameter value used), design (reference source creel_design), conf_level (numeric), by_vars (character vector grouping variable names NULL).

title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
text: Trip Type Selection (use_trips): trip_status provided, use_trips parameter controls trips used estimation. default use_trips = "complete" filters complete trips , following roving-access design best practices (Pollock et al. 1994). Complete trip interviews taken trip completion avoid length--stay bias. Setting use_trips = "incomplete" filters incomplete trips automatically uses MOR estimator. Incomplete trip estimation diagnostic/research mode requires validation (see Phase 19 validate_incomplete_trips). Setting use_trips = "diagnostic" runs complete incomplete trip estimation returns comparison object difference metrics interpretation guidance. Diagnostic mode requires trip types present data. trip_status provided, use_trips ignored perfect backward compatibility v0.2.0. Ratio--Means (default): CPUE estimated ratio total catch total effort. appropriate estimator complete trip interviews (interview trip end). function uses survey::svyratio() internally, correctly accounts correlation catch effort variance estimation. Mean--Ratios (MOR): estimator = "mor", CPUE estimated mean individual catch/effort ratios. statistically appropriate estimator incomplete trip interviews (interview trip). MOR automatically filters incomplete trips requires trip_status field. function uses survey::svymean() individual ratios. Trip Truncation: short incomplete trips can produce extreme catch/effort ratios dominate variance estimation. Following Hoenig et al. (1997), default truncate_at = 0.5 hours (30 minutes) excludes trips shorter threshold MOR estimation. survey design rebuilt truncated sample correct variance computation. Set truncate_at = NULL disable truncation (research mode ). Truncation applies MOR estimator; ratio--means ignores parameter. function performs sample size validation estimation: errors n < 10 (ungrouped group), warns 10 <= n < 30. MOR, validation uses post-truncation sample size. follows best practices ratio estimation stability. grouped estimation used (NULL), survey::svyby() correctly accounts domain estimation variance. Variance estimation methods: "taylor" (default): Taylor linearization, computationally efficient appropriate smooth statistics like ratios. "bootstrap": Bootstrap resampling 500 replicates. Appropriate verifying Taylor assumptions. "jackknife": Jackknife resampling (automatic JKn JK1 selection based design). Alternative resampling method.

title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
text: called sectioned design, .lake_total row produced. Catch rates (fish per angler-hour) additive across sections. Lake-wide catch rate requires separate unsectioned call full design. See estimate_total_catch() lake-wide total catch estimation.

title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
text: Complete Trip Percentage Threshold: package option tidycreel.min_complete_pct controls threshold complete trip percentage warnings (default: 0.10 = 10\ percentage complete trips falls threshold, warning issued referencing Pollock et al. roving-access design best practices. Users can set custom threshold session: options(tidycreel.min_complete_pct = 0.05) default 10\ scientifically valid estimation. Lowering threshold appropriate special cases documented justification. Warnings help ensure data quality guide users toward diagnostic validation complete trip samples insufficient.

title: Estimate CPUE (Catch Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_catch_rate
code: # Basic ungrouped CPUE calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(rep(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04"), each = 10)),   catch_total = rpois(40, lambda = 3),   hours_fished = runif(40, min = 1, max = 6),   trip_status = rep(c("complete", "incomplete"), each = 20),   trip_duration = runif(40, min = 1, max = 6) )  design_with_interviews <- add_interviews(design, interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 3 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> ℹ Added 40 interviews: 20 complete (50%), 20 incomplete (50%) result <- estimate_catch_rate(design_with_interviews) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=20, 50% of 40 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 20. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. print(result) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Ratio-of-Means CPUE #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     1.06 0.171    0.722     1.39    20  # Grouped by day_type result_grouped <- estimate_catch_rate(design_with_interviews, by = day_type) #> Warning: ! Only 0.0% of interviews are complete trips (threshold: 10%) #> ℹ Pollock et al. recommends >=10% complete trips for valid estimation #> ℹ Consider use_trips='diagnostic' to validate incomplete trip estimates #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=20, 50% of 40 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size in 1 group: #> • Group day_type=weekday: n=20 #> ! Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30 per group. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. print(result_grouped) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Ratio-of-Means CPUE #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #> Grouped by: day_type #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   day_type estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 weekday      1.06 0.171    0.722     1.39    20  # Custom confidence level result_90 <- estimate_catch_rate(design_with_interviews, conf_level = 0.90) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=20, 50% of 40 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 20. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30.  # Bootstrap variance estimation result_boot <- estimate_catch_rate(design_with_interviews, variance = "bootstrap") #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=20, 50% of 40 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 20. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30.  # Mean-of-ratios for incomplete trips result_mor <- estimate_catch_rate(design_with_interviews, estimator = "mor") #> ℹ Using incomplete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=20, 50% of 40 interviews) #> Warning: ! MOR estimator for incomplete trips. Complete trips preferred. #> ℹ Using MOR with n=20 incomplete of 20 total interviews. #> ℹ Incomplete trips may have length-of-stay bias (Pollock et al.). #> ℹ Validate incomplete estimates with `validate_incomplete_trips()` (Phase 19). #> ℹ MOR truncation: 0 trips excluded (all >= 0.5 hours) #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 20. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30.  # Mean-of-ratios with custom truncation threshold result_mor_1h <- estimate_catch_rate(design_with_interviews, estimator = "mor", truncate_at = 1.0) #> ℹ Using incomplete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=20, 50% of 40 interviews) #> Warning: ! MOR estimator for incomplete trips. Complete trips preferred. #> ℹ Using MOR with n=20 incomplete of 20 total interviews. #> ℹ Incomplete trips may have length-of-stay bias (Pollock et al.). #> ℹ Validate incomplete estimates with `validate_incomplete_trips()` (Phase 19). #> ℹ MOR truncation: 0 trips excluded (all >= 1 hours) #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 20. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30.

what: Estimate total effort from a creel survey design — estimate_effort
title: Estimate total effort from a creel survey design — estimate_effort
text: Computes total effort estimates standard errors confidence intervals creel survey design attached count data. Wraps survey::svytotal() (ungrouped) survey::svyby() (grouped) Tier 2 validation domain-specific output formatting.

title: Estimate total effort from a creel survey design — estimate_effort
code: estimate_effort(   design,   by = NULL,   variance = "taylor",   conf_level = 0.95,   verbose = FALSE,   aggregate_sections = TRUE,   method = "correlated",   missing_sections = "warn" )

title: Estimate total effort from a creel survey design — estimate_effort
text: design creel_design object counts attached via add_counts. design must survey object constructed. Optional tidy selector grouping variables. Accepts bare column names (e.g., = day_type), multiple columns (e.g., = c(day_type, location)), tidyselect helpers (e.g., = starts_with("day")). NULL (default), computes single total estimate across observations. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method. Options: "taylor" (default, Taylor linearization), "bootstrap" (bootstrap resampling 500 replicates), "jackknife" (jackknife resampling, automatic JKn/JK1 selection). conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals (default: 0.95 95% confidence intervals). Must 0 1. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informational message identifying estimator path used. Default FALSE transparent dispatch. aggregate_sections Logical. TRUE (default), .lake_total row appended aggregating across sections. Ignored non-sectioned designs. method Character string specifying lake-wide total SE computed aggregate_sections = TRUE. "correlated" (default) uses svyby(covmat=TRUE) + svycontrast() covariance-aware aggregation (recommended shared-calendar NGPC designs). "independent" uses Cochran 5.2 sqrt(sum(SE_h^2)) documented approximation genuinely independent section designs. Ignored non-sectioned designs. missing_sections Character string controlling behavior registered section count observations. "warn" (default) emits cli_warn() inserts NA row data_available = FALSE. "error" aborts cli_abort(). Ignored non-sectioned designs.

title: Estimate total effort from a creel survey design — estimate_effort
text: creel_estimates S3 object (list) components: estimates (tibble estimate, se, se_between, se_within, ci_lower, ci_upper, n columns, plus grouping columns specified), method (character: "total"), variance_method (character: reflects variance parameter value used), design (reference source creel_design), conf_level (numeric), by_vars (character vector grouping variable names NULL). se_between -day standard error survey::svytotal() (equals se single count recorded per PSU). se_within within-day standard error Rasmussen two-stage formula; zero single count recorded per PSU nonzero count_time_col supplied add_counts(). bus-route designs, "site_contributions" attribute also present containing per-site e_i, pi_i, e_i_over_pi_i columns.

title: Estimate total effort from a creel survey design — estimate_effort
text: function performs Tier 2 validation estimation, issuing warnings (errors) : zero values count variables, negative values count variables, sparse strata (< 3 observations). grouped estimation used (NULL), additional warnings issued sparse groups (< 3 observations per group level). Grouped estimation uses survey::svyby() internally, correctly accounts domain estimation variance. different naive subsetting, underestimate variance. Variance estimation methods: "taylor" (default): Taylor linearization, computationally efficient appropriate smooth statistics. recommended default. "bootstrap": Bootstrap resampling 500 replicates. Appropriate non-smooth statistics verifying Taylor assumptions. computationally intensive Taylor. "jackknife": Jackknife resampling (automatic JKn JK1 selection based design). Alternative resampling method, deterministic unlike bootstrap.

title: Estimate total effort from a creel survey design — estimate_effort
code: # Basic ungrouped usage calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  counts <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend"),   effort_hours = c(15, 23, 45, 52) )  design_with_counts <- add_counts(design, counts) #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability result <- estimate_effort(design_with_counts) #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 observations #> • Stratum weekend: 2 observations #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. print(result) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1      135  10.6       10.6         0     89.3     181.     4  # Grouped by day_type result_grouped <- estimate_effort(design_with_counts, by = day_type) #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 observations #> • Stratum weekend: 2 observations #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> Warning: 2 groups have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Group day_type=weekday: 2 observations #> • Group day_type=weekend: 2 observations #> ! Sparse groups produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse groups or collecting more data. print(result_grouped) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #> Grouped by: day_type #>  #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   day_type estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 weekday        38     8          8         0     3.58     72.4     2 #> 2 weekend        97     7          7         0    66.9     127.      2  # Note: Multiple grouping variables are supported if present in the data # For example: by = c(day_type, location)  # Custom confidence level result_90 <- estimate_effort(design_with_counts, conf_level = 0.90) #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 observations #> • Stratum weekend: 2 observations #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data.  # Bootstrap variance estimation result_boot <- estimate_effort(design_with_counts, variance = "bootstrap") #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 observations #> • Stratum weekend: 2 observations #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. print(result_boot) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Bootstrap #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1      135  9.86       9.86         0     92.6     177.     4  # Jackknife variance estimation result_jk <- estimate_effort(design_with_counts, variance = "jackknife") #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 observations #> • Stratum weekend: 2 observations #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. print(result_jk) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total #> Variance: Jackknife #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   estimate    se se_between se_within ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1      135  10.6       10.6         0     89.3     181.     4  # Grouped estimation with bootstrap variance result_grouped_boot <- estimate_effort(design_with_counts, by = day_type, variance = "bootstrap") #> Warning: 2 strata have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 observations #> • Stratum weekend: 2 observations #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> Warning: 2 groups have fewer than 3 observations: #> • Group day_type=weekday: 2 observations #> • Group day_type=weekend: 2 observations #> !
