what: GLMM-based aerial effort estimation with diurnal correction — estimate_effort_aerial_glmm
title: GLMM-based aerial effort estimation with diurnal correction — estimate_effort_aerial_glmm
text: Estimates total angler effort aerial creel surveys using generalized linear mixed model (GLMM), following approach Askey et al. (2018). flights occur non-random times day, simple scaling instantaneous counts can - -estimate daily effort. function fits negative-binomial GLMM (user-specified family) model angler counts change day, integrates fitted diurnal curve fishing day obtain bias-corrected effort estimate. default model quadratic temporal model Askey (2018): count ~ poly(time_col, 2) + (1 | date), fitted via lme4::glmer.nb(). Variance propagated via delta method (default) parametric bootstrap (lme4::bootMer()).

title: GLMM-based aerial effort estimation with diurnal correction — estimate_effort_aerial_glmm
code: estimate_effort_aerial_glmm(   design,   time_col,   formula = NULL,   family = NULL,   boot = FALSE,   nboot = 500L,   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: GLMM-based aerial effort estimation with diurnal correction — estimate_effort_aerial_glmm
text: design creel_design() object design_type == "aerial" counts attached via add_counts(). counts data must contain time--flight column specified time_col. time_col Unquoted name numeric column design$counts recording hour aerial overflight (e.g., time_of_flight). formula Optional. formula GLMM, passed directly lme4::glmer.nb() lme4::glmer(). NULL (default), Askey (2018) quadratic formula used: count ~ poly(time_col, 2) + (1 | date). family Optional. family object character string specifying GLM family. NULL "negbin" (default), lme4::glmer.nb() used. Otherwise, lme4::glmer() called specified family. boot Logical. TRUE, use lme4::bootMer() parametric bootstrap confidence intervals instead delta method. Default FALSE. nboot Integer. Number bootstrap replicates boot = TRUE. Default 500L. conf_level Numeric confidence level CI. Default 0.95.

title: GLMM-based aerial effort estimation with diurnal correction — estimate_effort_aerial_glmm
text: creel_estimates object : estimate: total angler effort integrated fishing day se: standard error (delta method bootstrap SD) se_between: se (fixed-effect SE component) se_within: always NA_real_ — Rasmussen within-day decomposition performed GLMM estimates ci_lower, ci_upper: confidence interval bounds n: number count observations used fit model method: "aerial_glmm_total"

title: GLMM-based aerial effort estimation with diurnal correction — estimate_effort_aerial_glmm
text: Askey, P.J., et al. (2018). Correcting non-random flight timing aerial creel surveys using generalized linear mixed model. North American Journal Fisheries Management, 38, 1204-1215. doi:10.1002/nafm.10010

title: GLMM-based aerial effort estimation with diurnal correction — estimate_effort_aerial_glmm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(example_aerial_glmm_counts)  aerial_cal <- unique(example_aerial_glmm_counts[, c("date", "day_type")]) aerial_cal <- aerial_cal[order(aerial_cal$date), ] design <- creel_design(   aerial_cal,   date = date,   strata = day_type,   survey_type = "aerial",   h_open = 14 ) design <- add_counts(design, example_aerial_glmm_counts)  # Default Askey quadratic model with delta-method SE result <- estimate_effort_aerial_glmm(design, time_col = time_of_flight) print(result)  # Bootstrap CIs (slower) result_boot <- estimate_effort_aerial_glmm(   design,   time_col = time_of_flight,   boot = TRUE,   nboot = 100L ) print(result_boot) } # }

title: Estimate harvest (HPUE: Harvest Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_harvest_rate

HPUE measures rate kept fish (harvest) per unit effort, distinguished total catch rate (CPUE includes kept released fish).

code: estimate_harvest_rate(   design,   by = NULL,   variance = "taylor",   conf_level = 0.95,   verbose = FALSE,   use_trips = NULL,   missing_sections = "warn" )

title: Estimate harvest (HPUE: Harvest Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_harvest_rate
text: design creel_design object interviews attached via add_interviews. design must interview survey object constructed harvest, catch, effort columns. Optional tidy selector grouping variables. Accepts bare column names (e.g., = day_type), multiple columns (e.g., = c(day_type, location)), tidyselect helpers (e.g., = starts_with("day")). NULL (default), computes single HPUE estimate across interviews. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method. Options: "taylor" (default, Taylor linearization), "bootstrap" (bootstrap resampling 500 replicates), "jackknife" (jackknife resampling, automatic JKn/JK1 selection). conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals (default: 0.95 95% confidence intervals). Must 0 1. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informational message identifying estimator path used. Default FALSE. use_trips Character string specifying trip type use bus-route estimation. One "complete" (default), "incomplete" (pi_i-weighted MOR), "diagnostic" (). Ignored non-bus-route designs. missing_sections Character string controlling behavior registered section interview observations. "warn" (default) emits cli_warn() inserts NA row data_available = FALSE. "error" aborts cli_abort(). Ignored non-sectioned designs.

title: Estimate harvest (HPUE: Harvest Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_harvest_rate
text: creel_estimates S3 object (list) components: estimates (tibble estimate, se, ci_lower, ci_upper, n columns, plus grouping columns specified), method (character: "ratio--means-hpue", "-per-angler" suffix normalized), variance_method (character: reflects variance parameter value used), design (reference source creel_design), conf_level (numeric), by_vars (character vector grouping variable names NULL). bus-route designs, "site_contributions" attribute also present.

title: Estimate harvest (HPUE: Harvest Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_harvest_rate
text: HPUE estimated ratio total harvest (kept fish) total effort (ratio--means estimator). appropriate estimator average harvest rates trip lengths (effort) vary. function uses survey::svyratio() internally, correctly accounts correlation harvest effort variance estimation. HPUE always less equal CPUE data, since harvest (kept fish) subset total catch. function performs sample size validation estimation: errors n < 10 (ungrouped group), warns 10 <= n < 30. follows best practices ratio estimation stability. grouped estimation used (NULL), survey::svyby() svyratio correctly accounts domain estimation variance. Variance estimation methods: "taylor" (default): Taylor linearization, computationally efficient appropriate smooth statistics like ratios. "bootstrap": Bootstrap resampling 500 replicates. Appropriate verifying Taylor assumptions. "jackknife": Jackknife resampling (automatic JKn JK1 selection based design). Alternative resampling method.

title: Estimate harvest (HPUE: Harvest Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_harvest_rate
text: called sectioned design, .lake_total row produced. Harvest rates (fish per angler-hour) additive across sections. Lake-wide harvest rate requires separate unsectioned call.

title: Estimate harvest (HPUE: Harvest Per Unit Effort) from a creel survey design — estimate_harvest_rate
code: # Basic ungrouped HPUE calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekday", "weekend", "weekend") ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  set.seed(123) interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(rep(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04"), each = 10)),   catch_total = rpois(40, lambda = 3),   hours_fished = runif(40, min = 1, max = 6),   trip_status = rep(c("complete", "incomplete"), each = 20),   trip_duration = runif(40, min = 1, max = 6) ) # Harvest is subset of catch (kept fish) interviews$catch_kept <- pmax(0, interviews$catch_total - rbinom(40, size = 2, prob = 0.3))  design_with_interviews <- add_interviews(design, interviews,   catch = catch_total,   harvest = catch_kept,   effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 3 interviews have zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> ℹ Added 40 interviews: 20 complete (50%), 20 incomplete (50%) result <- estimate_harvest_rate(design_with_interviews) print(result) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Ratio-of-Means HPUE #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1    0.923 0.121    0.686     1.16    40  # Grouped by day_type result_grouped <- estimate_harvest_rate(design_with_interviews, by = day_type) #> Warning: Small sample size in 2 groups: #> • Group day_type=weekday: n=20 #> • Group day_type=weekend: n=20 #> ! Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30 per group. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. print(result_grouped) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Ratio-of-Means HPUE #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #> Grouped by: day_type #>  #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   day_type estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 weekday     0.917 0.202    0.521     1.31    20 #> 2 weekend     0.929 0.142    0.650     1.21    20  # Custom confidence level result_90 <- estimate_harvest_rate(design_with_interviews, conf_level = 0.90)  # Bootstrap variance estimation result_boot <- estimate_harvest_rate(design_with_interviews, variance = "bootstrap")  # Verbose dispatch message (shows which estimator was used for bus-route designs) # result_verbose <- estimate_harvest_rate(design, verbose = TRUE)

code: library(tidycreel) data(example_calendar) data(example_counts) data(example_interviews) data(example_catch)  design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- add_counts(design, example_counts) #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability design <- add_interviews(design, example_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%) design <- add_catch(design, example_catch,   catch_uid = interview_id,   interview_uid = interview_id,   species = species,   count = count,   catch_type = catch_type )  # Overall release rate (all species combined) rpue <- estimate_release_rate(design) #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 22. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. print(rpue) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: ratio-of-means-rpue #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate     se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1    0.602 0.0956    0.414    0.789    22  # Per-species release rates rpue_by_species <- estimate_release_rate(design, by = species) #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! what: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
text: Computes total catch estimates multiplying effort × CPUE variance propagation via delta method. Requires creel design count data (effort estimation) interview data (CPUE estimation).

title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
code: estimate_total_catch(   design,   by = NULL,   variance = "taylor",   conf_level = 0.95,   aggregate_sections = TRUE,   missing_sections = "warn",   verbose = FALSE )

title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
text: design creel_design object counts (via add_counts) interviews (via add_interviews) attached. count interview survey objects must exist. Optional tidy selector grouping variables. specified, must match across effort CPUE estimates (calendar strata interview variables). Accepts bare column names, multiple columns, tidyselect helpers. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method: "taylor" (default), "bootstrap", "jackknife". Applied effort CPUE estimation, combined via delta method. conf_level Numeric confidence level (default: 0.95) aggregate_sections Logical. design created add_sections, .lake_total row appended sums per-section estimates? Default TRUE. Set FALSE return per-section rows without lake total. missing_sections Character(1). Action registered section absent either count data interview data: "warn" (default) inserts NA row data_available = FALSE, "error" raises hard error. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informational message identifying estimator path used. Default FALSE.

title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
text: creel_estimates S3 object method = "product-total-catch". bus-route designs, returns bus-route HT estimate method = "total" "site_contributions" attribute. sectioned designs, returns per-section rows plus (default) .lake_total row. lake-wide total computed sum(TC_i) sections, never E_total * CPUE_pooled.

title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
text: Total catch computed Effort × CPUE. Variance propagated using delta method, accounts uncertainty estimates. formula independent estimates approximately: $$Var(E \times C) \approx E^2 \cdot Var(C) + C^2 \cdot Var(E)$$ function uses survey::svycontrast() compute variance automatically via symbolic differentiation Taylor series approximation. Sectioned designs: add_sections called design, section estimated independently using count survey (via rebuild_counts_survey) interview survey (via rebuild_interview_survey). lake-wide total arithmetic sum sum(TC_i), E_total * CPUE_pooled. lake-wide SE uses zero-covariance assumption: sqrt(sum(se_i^2)). Cross-section covariance count-based effort interview-based CPUE designs identified therefore assumed zero. Design compatibility requirements: Count data must attached via add_counts() effort estimation Interview data must attached via add_interviews() CPUE estimation Grouped estimation requires identical grouping variables estimates Calendar stratification must shared counts interviews

title: Estimate total catch by combining effort and CPUE — estimate_total_catch
code: library(tidycreel) data(example_calendar) data(example_counts) data(example_interviews)  # Create design with both counts and interviews design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) design <- add_counts(design, example_counts) #> Warning: No weights or probabilities supplied, assuming equal probability design <- add_interviews(design, example_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%)  # Estimate total catch total_catch <- estimate_total_catch(design) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=17, 77.3% of 22 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 17. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. print(total_catch) #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Total Catch (Effort × CPUE) #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate    se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1     851.  52.0     749.     953.    17  # Compare components effort_est <- estimate_effort(design) cpue_est <- estimate_catch_rate(design) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=17, 77.3% of 22 interviews) [default] #> Warning: Small sample size for CPUE estimation. #> ! Sample size is 17. Ratio estimates are more stable with n >= 30. #> ℹ Variance estimates may be unstable with n < 30. # total_catch$estimates$estimate approximately equals effort_est * cpue_est  # Note: Grouped estimation requires n >= 10 per group # Check sample sizes before grouping: # table(design$interviews$day_type) # total_catch_by_type <- estimate_total_catch(design, by = day_type)  # Verbose dispatch message (shows which estimator was used for bus-route designs) # result_verbose <- estimate_total_catch(design, verbose = TRUE)

what: Estimate total extrapolated release by combining effort and release rate — estimate_total_release
title: Estimate total extrapolated release by combining effort and release rate — estimate_total_release
text: Computes total release estimates multiplying effort x RPUE variance propagation via delta method. Requires creel design count data (effort estimation), interview data (effort), catch data (via add_catch) containing released records.

title: Estimate total extrapolated release by combining effort and release rate — estimate_total_release

title: Estimate total extrapolated release by combining effort and release rate — estimate_total_release
text: design creel_design object counts (via add_counts), interviews (via add_interviews), catch data (via add_catch) attached. Catch data must include records catch_type = "released". Optional tidy selector grouping variables. Accepts bare column names (e.g., = day_type, = species), multiple columns, tidyselect helpers. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method: "taylor" (default), "bootstrap", "jackknife". Applied effort release rate estimation, combined via delta method. conf_level Numeric confidence level (default: 0.95). aggregate_sections Logical. design created add_sections, .lake_total row appended sums per-section estimates? Default TRUE. Set FALSE return per-section rows without lake total. missing_sections Character(1). Action registered section absent either count data interview data: "warn" (default) inserts NA row data_available = FALSE, "error" raises hard error.

title: Estimate total extrapolated release by combining effort and release rate — estimate_total_release
text: creel_estimates S3 object method = "product-total-release". Estimates tibble columns: estimate, se, ci_lower, ci_upper, n (plus grouping columns).

title: Estimate total extrapolated release by combining effort and release rate — estimate_total_release
text: Total release computed Effort x RPUE. Variance propagated using delta method: Var(E x R) = E^2 * Var(R) + R^2 * Var(E). Sectioned designs: add_sections called design, section estimated independently. lake-wide total sum(TR_i), E_total * RPUE_pooled. lake-wide SE uses zero-covariance assumption: sqrt(sum(se_i^2)).

what: Example multi-flight aerial count data for GLMM effort estimation — example_aerial_glmm_counts
title: Example multi-flight aerial count data for GLMM effort estimation — example_aerial_glmm_counts
text: Simulated instantaneous angler counts aerial overflights Nebraska reservoir, designed demonstrate GLMM-based effort estimation following Askey (2018). Contains 48 rows: 12 survey days 4 overflights per day fixed hours (07:00, 10:00, 13:00, 16:00). Counts follow diurnal curve (low dawn, peak mid-morning, lower afternoon) day-level Poisson variability day random intercept.

title: Example multi-flight aerial count data for GLMM effort estimation — example_aerial_glmm_counts
code: example_aerial_glmm_counts

title: Example multi-flight aerial count data for GLMM effort estimation — example_aerial_glmm_counts
text: data frame 48 rows 4 columns: date Survey date (Date class), 12 days spaced 3 days apart starting 2024-06-03. day_type Day type stratum: "weekday" "weekend", derived calendar date. n_anglers Instantaneous angler count one aerial overflight (integer). Follows diurnal curve day-level random effects. time_of_flight Hour aerial overflight (numeric). One 7.0, 10.0, 13.0, 16.0.

title: Example multi-flight aerial count data for GLMM effort estimation — example_aerial_glmm_counts
text: Askey, P.J., et al. (2018). Correcting non-random flight timing aerial creel surveys using generalized linear mixed model. North American Journal Fisheries Management, 38, 1204-1215. doi:10.1002/nafm.10010

title: Example multi-flight aerial count data for GLMM effort estimation — example_aerial_glmm_counts
code: data(example_aerial_glmm_counts) head(example_aerial_glmm_counts) #>         date day_type n_anglers time_of_flight #> 1 2024-06-03  weekday         3              7 #> 2 2024-06-03  weekday        30             10 #> 3 2024-06-03  weekday        65             13 #> 4 2024-06-03  weekday        50             16 #> 5 2024-06-06  weekday         5              7 #> 6 2024-06-06  weekday        15             10  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Build an aerial design and estimate effort with GLMM correction aerial_cal <- data.frame(   date = unique(example_aerial_glmm_counts$date),   day_type = unique(example_aerial_glmm_counts[, c("date", "day_type")])[["day_type"]],   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) design <- creel_design(   aerial_cal,   date = date,   strata = day_type,   survey_type = "aerial",   h_open = 14 ) design <- add_counts(design, example_aerial_glmm_counts) result <- estimate_effort_aerial_glmm(design, time_col = time_of_flight) print(result) } # }

code: data(example_camera_counts) data(example_camera_interviews)  # Build a calendar that spans all camera dataset dates cam_dates <- sort(unique(c(   example_camera_counts$date,   example_camera_interviews$date ))) cam_cal <- data.frame(   date = cam_dates,   day_type = ifelse(     weekdays(cam_dates) %in% c("Saturday", "Sunday"),     "weekend", "weekday"   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) design <- creel_design(   cam_cal,   date = date, strata = day_type,   survey_type = "camera",   camera_mode = "counter" ) counts_clean <- subset(example_camera_counts, camera_status == "operational") design <- suppressWarnings(add_counts(design, counts_clean)) design <- suppressWarnings(add_interviews(   design, example_camera_interviews,   catch = walleye, effort = hours_fished, trip_status = trip_status )) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 40 interviews: 40 complete (100%), 0 incomplete (0%) suppressWarnings(estimate_catch_rate(design)) #> ℹ Using complete trips for CPUE estimation #>   (n=40, 100% of 40 interviews) [default] #>  #> ── Creel Survey Estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Method: Ratio-of-Means CPUE #> Variance: Taylor linearization #> Confidence level: 95% #>  #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   estimate     se ci_lower ci_upper     n #>      <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1    0.453 0.0755    0.305    0.601    40

what: Flag outliers in a creel interview data column — flag_outliers
title: Flag outliers in a creel interview data column — flag_outliers
text: flag_outliers() identifies extreme values numeric column data frame using Tukey's IQR fence method. Flagged rows annotated is_outlier, outlier_reason, fence_low, fence_high columns. cli summary flagged rows emitted.

title: Flag outliers in a creel interview data column — flag_outliers
code: flag_outliers(data, col, k = 1.5, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Flag outliers in a creel interview data column — flag_outliers
text: data data.frame containing column check. col Bare column name (unquoted) check outliers. k Numeric IQR multiplier (default: 1.5). Larger values produce wider fences fewer flags. Tukey's standard values 1.5 (mild outliers) 3.0 (extreme outliers). na.rm Logical. Remove NA values computing quantiles (default: TRUE).

title: Flag outliers in a creel interview data column — flag_outliers
text: input data four additional columns appended: is_outlier Logical — TRUE row outside fence. outlier_reason Character — brief description flagged (e.g. "fence_high (23.5)"), "" flagged. fence_low Numeric — lower fence value (rows). NA n < 4. fence_high Numeric — upper fence value (rows). NA n < 4.

title: Flag outliers in a creel interview data column — flag_outliers
text: Method: Tukey's IQR fence. $$\text{fence\_low} = Q_1 - k \times IQR$$ $$\text{fence\_high} = Q_3 + k \times IQR$$ Values fence_low fence_high flagged. n < 4, insufficient data estimate IQR reliably; fences set NA rows flagged.

title: Flag outliers in a creel interview data column — flag_outliers
code: df <- data.frame(   interview_id = 1:8,   effort = c(1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 1.8, 1.2, 1.9, 2.1, 15.0) ) flag_outliers(df, col = effort) #> ℹ 1 of 8 values flagged as outliers in effort (k = 1.5, fence: [0.525, 2.925]). #>   interview_id effort is_outlier           outlier_reason fence_low fence_high #> 1            1    1.0      FALSE                              0.525      2.925 #> 2            2    1.5      FALSE                              0.525      2.925 #> 3            3    2.0      FALSE                              0.525      2.925 #> 4            4    1.8      FALSE                              0.525      2.925 #> 5            5    1.2      FALSE                              0.525      2.925 #> 6            6    1.9      FALSE                              0.525      2.925 #> 7            7    2.1      FALSE                              0.525      2.925 #> 8            8   15.0       TRUE above fence_high (2.925)     0.525      2.925

what: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
text: Converts creel schedule calendar circuit definitions sampling frame tibble inclusion_prob p_period columns ready creel_design(survey_type = "bus_route"). Inclusion probability formula: inclusion_prob = p_site * p_period p_period = crew / n_circuits. n_circuits number distinct circuit values sampling_frame (1 circuit NULL). crew number field crews deployed simultaneously.

title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
code: generate_bus_schedule(   schedule,   sampling_frame,   site,   p_site,   circuit = NULL,   crew,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
text: schedule creel_schedule tibble generate_schedule(). Currently unused computation required ensure caller built valid schedule constructing sampling frame. sampling_frame data frame site p_site columns (optionally circuit). site Column sampling_frame giving site identifiers (tidy selector: bare name, quoted string, tidyselect helper). p_site Column sampling_frame giving per-site selection probability within circuit. Values must sum 1.0 per circuit (tolerance 1e-6). circuit Optional column giving circuit assignment. NULL, sites treated single circuit. crew Integer scalar: number crews field simultaneously. seed Optional integer seed (reserved future randomised designs; currently unused function deterministic).

title: Generate a bus-route sampling frame — generate_bus_schedule
text: tibble: sampling_frame columns plus p_period inclusion_prob. inclusion_prob = p_site * p_period.

what: Generate within-day count time windows — generate_count_times
title: Generate within-day count time windows — generate_count_times
text: Generates count time windows creel survey day using one three strategies: random (stratified random placement within equal-width strata), systematic (random start first stratum fixed spacing thereafter, preferred per Pollock et al. 1994 Colorado CPW 2012), fixed (user-supplied non-overlapping windows).

title: Generate within-day count time windows — generate_count_times
code: generate_count_times(   start_time = NULL,   end_time = NULL,   strategy,   n_windows = NULL,   window_size = NULL,   min_gap = NULL,   fixed_windows = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate within-day count time windows — generate_count_times
text: start_time Character. Survey-day start time "HH:MM" format. Required strategy = "random" "systematic". end_time Character. Survey-day end time "HH:MM" format. Required strategy = "random" "systematic". strategy Character scalar. One "random", "systematic", "fixed". n_windows Positive integer. Number count time windows. Required strategy = "random" "systematic". total span (end_time - start_time minutes) must evenly divisible n_windows. window_size Positive integer. Duration count window minutes. Required strategy = "random" "systematic". min_gap Non-negative integer. Minimum gap (minutes) windows. Required strategy = "random" "systematic". window_size + min_gap must exceed stratum width (total_span / n_windows). fixed_windows data frame start_time end_time columns (character "HH:MM"). Required strategy = "fixed". Windows must non-overlapping. seed Integer seed reproducible window placement. Passed withr::with_seed(). Applies "random" "systematic" strategies. effect "fixed" strategy.

title: Generate within-day count time windows — generate_count_times
text: creel_schedule data frame columns: start_time (character "HH:MM"): Window start time. end_time   (character "HH:MM"): Window end time. window_id  (integer, 1-based, ordered start time): Window index.

title: Generate within-day count time windows — generate_count_times
text: Output creel_schedule data frame compatible write_schedule(). Random strategy: n_windows strata equal length k = total_span / n_windows receives one window uniformly random start within [stratum_start, stratum_start + k - window_size]. Systematic strategy (recommended): single random start t1 drawn [start_min, start_min + k - window_size]; subsequent windows begin t1 + (-1) * k = 1, ..., n_windows. design described Pollock et al. (1994) recommended Colorado CPW (2012). Fixed strategy: Windows taken exactly supplied sorting start time. Overlapping windows trigger error.

title: Generate within-day count time windows — generate_count_times
code: # Random strategy generate_count_times(   start_time = "06:00", end_time = "14:00",   strategy = "random", n_windows = 4, window_size = 30, min_gap = 10,   seed = 42 ) #> # A creel_schedule: 4 rows x 3 cols (NA days, 1 periods) #> (no date column to render calendar)  # Systematic strategy (preferred; Pollock et al. 1994) generate_count_times(   start_time = "06:00", end_time = "14:00",   strategy = "systematic", n_windows = 4, window_size = 30, min_gap = 10,   seed = 42 ) #> # A creel_schedule: 4 rows x 3 cols (NA days, 1 periods) #> (no date column to render calendar)  # Fixed strategy fw <- data.frame(   start_time = c("07:00", "09:00", "11:00"),   end_time = c("07:30", "09:30", "11:30"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) generate_count_times(strategy = "fixed", fixed_windows = fw) #> # A creel_schedule: 3 rows x 3 cols (NA days, 1 periods) #> (no date column to render calendar)

what: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
text: Generates stratified random sampling calendar creel survey season. season divided weekday weekend strata, days randomly selected within stratum. Output creel_schedule tibble ready pass creel_design().

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
code: generate_schedule(   start_date,   end_date,   n_periods,   n_days = NULL,   sampling_rate = NULL,   period_labels = NULL,   expand_periods = TRUE,   include_all = FALSE,   ordered_periods = FALSE,   period_intensity = NULL,   seed )

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
text: start_date Character Date. First day survey season (ISO 8601 "YYYY-MM-DD"). end_date Character Date. Last day survey season (ISO 8601 "YYYY-MM-DD"). n_periods Integer. Number sampling periods per day. n_days Named integer vector days sample per stratum (e.g., c(weekday = 20, weekend = 10)), scalar applied uniformly strata. Mutually exclusive sampling_rate. sampling_rate Named numeric vector sampling fractions per stratum (e.g., c(weekday = 0.3, weekend = 0.6)), scalar applied uniformly strata. Mutually exclusive n_days. period_labels Optional character vector length n_periods human-readable period names. supplied, period_id character (ordered factor ordered_periods = TRUE). expand_periods Logical (default TRUE). TRUE, output one row per sampled day x period (nrow = sampled_days * n_periods). FALSE, output one row per sampled day period_id omitted. include_all Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, season dates returned sampled logical column. FALSE, sampled dates returned. ordered_periods Logical (default FALSE). TRUE period_labels supplied, period_id ordered factor preserving label order. period_intensity yet implemented. Must NULL. seed Integer seed reproducible random day selection. Uses withr::with_seed() avoid mutating global RNG state.

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
text: creel_schedule data frame columns: date (Date): Sampled () dates. day_type (character): "weekday" "weekend". period_id (integer, character, ordered factor): Period within day. Absent expand_periods = FALSE. sampled (logical): Present include_all = TRUE.

title: Generate a creel survey sampling schedule — generate_schedule
code: # Basic schedule with stratified sampling rates sched <- generate_schedule(   start_date = "2024-06-01",   end_date = "2024-08-31",   n_periods = 2,   sampling_rate = c(weekday = 0.3, weekend = 0.6),   seed = 42 )  # Use result with creel_design() creel_design(sched, date = date, strata = day_type)

what: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: Returns enumeration count data (observed interviewed angler counts, expansion factor) interview record bus-route creel_design interviews attached via add_interviews. expansion factor \(n\_counted / n\_interviewed\) accounts anglers present site interviewed. used bus-route effort harvest estimation (Jones & Pollock (2012) Eq. 19.4 19.5).

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
code: get_enumeration_counts(design)

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: design creel_design object design_type = "bus_route" interview data attached via add_interviews.

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: data frame site identifier column, circuit identifier column, n_counted (resolved column name), n_interviewed (resolved column name), .expansion (n_counted / n_interviewed, NA n_interviewed = 0).

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
text: Jones, C. M., & Pollock, K. H. (2012). Recreational survey methods: estimating effort, harvest, abundance. . V. Zale, D. L. Parrish, & T. M. Sutton (Eds.), Fisheries Techniques (3rd ed., pp. 883–919). American Fisheries Society. Enumeration expansion factor used Eq. 19.4 19.5 bus-route effort harvest estimation.

title: Get enumeration counts from a bus-route creel design with interviews — get_enumeration_counts
code: cal <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-03", "2024-06-04", "2024-06-05", "2024-06-06")),   day_type = "weekday" ) sf <- data.frame(   site = c("A", "B"),   circuit = c("am", "am"),   p_site = c(0.6, 0.4),   p_period = rep(0.5, 2) ) design_br <- creel_design(   cal,   date = date, strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route", sampling_frame = sf,   site = site, circuit = circuit,   p_site = p_site, p_period = p_period ) interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   site = c("A", "B"), circuit = c("am", "am"),   catch_total = c(3L, 2L), hours_fished = c(2.0, 1.5),   trip_status = c("complete", "complete"),   trip_duration = c(2.0, 1.5),   n_counted = c(5L, 4L), n_interviewed = c(3L, 2L) ) design2 <- add_interviews(   design_br, interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status, trip_duration = trip_duration,   n_counted = n_counted, n_interviewed = n_interviewed ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 1 stratum has fewer than 3 interviews: #> • Stratum weekday: 2 interviews #> ! Sparse strata produce unstable variance estimates. #> ℹ Consider combining sparse strata or collecting more data. #> ℹ Added 2 interviews: 2 complete (100%), 0 incomplete (0%) get_enumeration_counts(design2) #>   site circuit n_counted n_interviewed .expansion #> 1    A      am         5             3   1.666667 #> 2    B      am         4             2   2.000000

what: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: Returns computed inclusion probabilities (\(\pi_i = p_{\text{site}} \times p_{\text{period}}\)) site-circuit combination bus-route creel design. inclusion probability represents two-stage sampling probability: probability particular site visited particular sampling period, combining site selection probability within circuit circuit (period) selection probability.

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
code: get_inclusion_probs(design)

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: design creel_design() object created survey_type = "bus_route".

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: data frame three columns: site identifier column, circuit identifier column, .pi_i (computed inclusion probability \(\pi_i = p_{\text{site}} \times p_{\text{period}}\) site-circuit unit). Column names site circuit match resolved column names original sampling frame (.circuit designs without explicit circuit column).

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
text: Jones, C. M., & Pollock, K. H. (2012). Recreational survey methods: estimating effort, harvest, abundance. . V. Zale, D. L. Parrish, & T. M. Sutton (Eds.), Fisheries Techniques (3rd ed., pp. 883–919). American Fisheries Society. Definition \(\pi_i\) two-stage bus-route sampling, used Eq. 19.4 19.5.

title: Get inclusion probabilities from a bus-route design — get_inclusion_probs
code: sf <- data.frame(   site = c("A", "B", "C"),   p_site = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),   p_period = rep(0.5, 3),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) cal <- data.frame(   date = as.Date("2024-06-01"),   day_type = "weekday",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) design <- creel_design(cal,   date = date, strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route", sampling_frame = sf,   site = site, p_site = p_site, p_period = p_period ) get_inclusion_probs(design) #>   site .circuit .pi_i #> 1    A .default  0.15 #> 2    B .default  0.20 #> 3    C .default  0.15

what: Extract the sampling frame from a bus-route creel design — get_sampling_frame
title: Extract the sampling frame from a bus-route creel design — get_sampling_frame
text: Returns sampling frame data frame stored bus-route creel_design object. data frame contains user's original columns plus precomputed .pi_i column (pi_i = p_site * p_period). Aborts informative error non-bus-route designs.

title: Extract the sampling frame from a bus-route creel design — get_sampling_frame
code: get_sampling_frame(design)

title: Extract the sampling frame from a bus-route creel design — get_sampling_frame
text: design creel_design object design_type = "bus_route".

title: Extract the sampling frame from a bus-route creel design — get_sampling_frame
text: data frame: sampling_frame stored design, addition .pi_i column (circuit omitted) .circuit column.

title: Extract the sampling frame from a bus-route creel design — get_sampling_frame
code: sf <- data.frame(   site     = c("A", "B", "C"),   p_site   = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),   p_period = 0.5 ) calendar <- data.frame(   date     = as.Date("2024-06-01"),   day_type = "weekday" ) design <- creel_design(calendar,   date = date,   strata = day_type,   survey_type = "bus_route",   sampling_frame = sf,   site = site,   p_site = p_site,   p_period = p_period ) get_sampling_frame(design) #>   site p_site p_period .circuit .pi_i #> 1    A    0.3      0.5 .default  0.15 #> 2    B    0.4      0.5 .default  0.20 #> 3    C    0.3      0.5 .default  0.15

what: Extract per-site effort contributions from a bus-route estimate — get_site_contributions
title: Extract per-site effort contributions from a bus-route estimate — get_site_contributions
text: Returns per-site calculation table (eᵢ, πᵢ, eᵢ/πᵢ) stored attribute effort estimate objects returned estimate_effort() bus-route survey designs. table enables traceability Horvitz-Thompson estimator (Jones & Pollock 2012, Eq. 19.4) supports validation published examples (Malvestuto 1996, Box 20.6).

title: Extract per-site effort contributions from a bus-route estimate — get_site_contributions
code: get_site_contributions(x)

title: Extract per-site effort contributions from a bus-route estimate — get_site_contributions
text: x creel_estimates object returned estimate_effort() bus-route design.

title: Extract per-site effort contributions from a bus-route estimate — get_site_contributions
text: tibble columns: site Site identifier (sampling frame) circuit Circuit identifier (sampling frame) e_i Enumeration-expanded effort site (effort * expansion) pi_i Inclusion probability site (p_site * p_period) e_i_over_pi_i Site contribution Horvitz-Thompson estimate

title: Extract per-site effort contributions from a bus-route estimate — get_site_contributions
text: Jones, C. M., & Pollock, K. H. (2012). Recreational survey methods: estimating effort, harvest, abundance. . V. Zale, D. L. Parrish, & T. M. Sutton (Eds.), Fisheries Techniques (3rd ed., pp. 883-919). American Fisheries Society.

what: Unified sample-size and power interface for creel surveys — power_creel
title: Unified sample-size and power interface for creel surveys — power_creel
text: single tidy entry point pre-survey sample-size planning wraps creel_n_effort(), creel_n_cpue(), creel_power() returns consistent tibble.

title: Unified sample-size and power interface for creel surveys — power_creel
code: power_creel(   mode = c("effort_n", "cpue_n", "power"),   target_rse = NULL,   strata = NULL,   N_h = NULL,   ybar_h = NULL,   s2_h = NULL,   cv_catch = NULL,   cv_effort = NULL,   rho = 0,   n = NULL,   cv_historical = NULL,   delta_pct = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "one.sided") )

title: Unified sample-size and power interface for creel surveys — power_creel
text: mode Character scalar. One "effort_n", "cpue_n", "power". Selects planning formula. target_rse Numeric scalar (0, 1]. Target relative standard error (= target CV). Required mode %% c("effort_n", "cpue_n"). strata Character vector stratum names. Required mode = "effort_n". Length must match N_h, ybar_h, s2_h. N_h Numeric vector. Total sampling days available per stratum. Required mode = "effort_n". ybar_h Numeric vector. Pilot mean effort per day per stratum. Required mode = "effort_n". s2_h Numeric vector. Pilot variance effort per day per stratum. Required mode = "effort_n". cv_catch Numeric scalar. Pilot CV catch per interview. Required mode %% c("cpue_n", "power"). cv_effort Numeric scalar. Pilot CV effort per interview. Required mode = "cpue_n". rho Numeric scalar [-1, 1]. Pilot correlation catch effort. Default 0 (conservative). Used mode %% c("cpue_n", "power"). n Integerish scalar. Sample size (interviews) mode = "power". cv_historical Numeric scalar. Historical CV CPUE mode = "power". NULL, cv_catch used proxy. delta_pct Numeric scalar (> 0). Fractional change detect. Required mode = "power". alpha Numeric scalar (0, 0.5]. Type error rate. Default 0.05. Used mode = "power". alternative Character. "two.sided" (default) "one.sided". Used mode = "power".

title: Unified sample-size and power interface for creel surveys — power_creel
text: tibble (data frame) columns varying mode: mode = "effort_n" (one row per stratum plus "total" row): stratum Stratum name. n_required Sampling days required. target_rse requested target RSE. mode = "cpue_n" (one row): n_required Interviews required. target_rse requested target RSE. cv_catch Input CV catch. cv_effort Input CV effort. rho Input correlation. mode = "power" (one row): power Estimated statistical power. n Input sample size. delta_pct Input fractional change. cv_historical Historical CV used. alpha Input significance level. alternative Input test direction.

title: Unified sample-size and power interface for creel surveys — power_creel
text: Three mode values supported: "effort_n" Required sampling days per stratum achieve target_rse effort estimate (calls creel_n_effort()). "cpue_n" Required interviews achieve target_rse CPUE estimate (calls creel_n_cpue()). "power" Statistical power detect fractional change CPUE given sample size (calls creel_power()).

title: Unified sample-size and power interface for creel surveys — power_creel
code: # Effort: sampling days needed for 20 percent RSE power_creel(   mode       = "effort_n",   target_rse = 0.20,   strata     = c("weekday", "weekend"),   N_h        = c(65, 28),   ybar_h     = c(50, 60),   s2_h       = c(400, 500) ) #>   stratum n_required target_rse #> 1 weekday          3        0.2 #> 2 weekend          2        0.2 #> 3   total          4        0.2  # CPUE: interviews needed for 20 percent RSE power_creel(   mode       = "cpue_n",   target_rse = 0.20,   cv_catch   = 0.8,   cv_effort  = 0.5 ) #>   n_required target_rse cv_catch cv_effort rho #> 1         23        0.2      0.8       0.5   0  # Power: detect a 20 percent change with n = 80 interviews power_creel(   mode          = "power",   n             = 80L,   cv_historical = 0.5,   delta_pct     = 0.20 ) #>       power  n delta_pct cv_historical alpha alternative #> 1 0.7156166 80       0.2           0.5  0.05   two.sided

what: Standardize species names to AFS codes — standardize_species
title: Standardize species names to AFS codes — standardize_species
text: Maps free-text species names data frame canonical American Fisheries Society (AFS) species codes, appending species_code column. Matching case-insensitive checks exact common names comma-separated aliases bundled package.  Values already look like known AFS code (-uppercase, 3 characters) passed directly.  Unmatched values left NA cli warning listing unrecognised inputs.

title: Standardize species names to AFS codes — standardize_species
code: standardize_species(   data,   species_col = "species",   lookup = "AFS",   fuzzy = TRUE,   keep_original = TRUE )

title: Standardize species names to AFS codes — standardize_species
text: data data frame containing species name column. species_col Character scalar naming column holds species names.  Default "species". lookup Character scalar identifying code system use. Currently "AFS" (default) supported; passing value raises error. fuzzy Logical.  TRUE (default), aliases (common abbreviations alternate names) also searched.  Set FALSE strict common-name matching . keep_original Logical.  TRUE (default), original species_col column preserved unchanged.  Set FALSE drop .

title: Standardize species names to AFS codes — standardize_species
text: data additional species_code character column appended. Unmatched rows receive NA_character_.

title: Standardize species names to AFS codes — standardize_species
code: interviews <- data.frame(   date    = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03")),   species = c("walleye", "Largemouth Bass", "UNKNOWN"),   kept    = c(2L, 1L, 0L) ) standardize_species(interviews) #> Warning: 1 species value(s) could not be matched to an #> AFS code and will be "NA": #> • "UNKNOWN" #>         date         species kept species_code #> 1 2024-06-01         walleye    2          WAE #> 2 2024-06-02 Largemouth Bass    1          LMB #> 3 2024-06-03         UNKNOWN    0         <NA>

what: Compute caught-while-sought (CWS) rates by group — summarize_cws_rates
title: Compute caught-while-sought (CWS) rates by group — summarize_cws_rates
text: Computes mean caught--sought rates (fish per angler-hour) anglers targeting species. interview, rate : caught_count / angler_effort caught_count total number fish caught species angler seeking, angler_effort angler-hours (effort x n_anglers, standardized design time add_interviews).

title: Compute caught-while-sought (CWS) rates by group — summarize_cws_rates
code: summarize_cws_rates(design, by = NULL, conf_level = 0.95)

title: Compute caught-while-sought (CWS) rates by group — summarize_cws_rates
text: design creel_design object interviews attached via add_interviews (species_sought) species catch data attached via add_catch. Optional tidy selector grouping columns design$interviews. Common choices: = species_sought (CWS-03), = c(month, species_sought) (CWS-02), = c(month, angler_type, species_sought) (CWS-01). NULL, returns single overall rate across interviews. conf_level Numeric confidence level t-interval. Default 0.95.

title: Compute caught-while-sought (CWS) rates by group — summarize_cws_rates
text: data.frame class c("creel_summary_cws_rates", "data.frame") columns: grouping columns (), N (integer, interviews per group), mean_rate (numeric, mean fish/angler-hour), se (numeric, standard error), ci_lower, ci_upper.

title: Compute caught-while-sought (CWS) rates by group — summarize_cws_rates
text: Interview-based summary, pressure-weighted. function computes simple arithmetic mean sampled interviews. apply survey weighting sampling effort effort stratum. pressure-weighted extrapolated estimates use estimate_catch_rate. catch filter ensures species angler targeting counted (.e., rows design$catch catch_type == "caught" species == species_sought).

title: Compute caught-while-sought (CWS) rates by group — summarize_cws_rates
code: data(example_calendar) data(example_interviews) data(example_catch) d <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type) d <- add_interviews(d, example_interviews,   catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished, harvest = catch_kept,   trip_status = trip_status, species_sought = species_sought ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%) d <- add_catch(d, example_catch,   catch_uid = interview_id, interview_uid = interview_id,   species = species, count = count, catch_type = catch_type ) summarize_cws_rates(d, by = species_sought) #>   species_sought  N mean_rate        se    ci_lower  ci_upper #> 1           bass  6 0.2083333 0.2083333 -0.32720455 0.7438712 #> 2        panfish  5 0.4666667 0.4666667 -0.82900772 1.7623410 #> 3        walleye 11 0.7835498 0.3359583  0.03498793 1.5321116

Common choices: = species_sought (HWS-03), = c(month, species_sought) (HWS-02), = c(month, angler_type, species_sought) (HWS-01). NULL, returns single overall rate across interviews. conf_level Numeric confidence level t-interval. Default 0.95.

title: Compute harvested-while-sought (HWS) rates by group — summarize_hws_rates

title: Compute length frequency distribution from creel interview data — summarize_length_freq

One "catch" (fish, harvest + release combined), "harvest" (kept fish ), "release" (released fish ). Default "catch". Optional tidy selector grouping columns design$lengths. Common choice: = species. title: tidycreel: Tidy Interface for Creel Survey Design and Analysis — tidycreel-package

text: Maintainer: Christopher Chizinski chris.chizinski@unl.edu (ORCID)

what: Validate creel survey data frames — validate_creel_data
title: Validate creel survey data frames — validate_creel_data
text: Runs field-level schema quality checks counts /interview data frames, returning tidy results tibble pass/warn/fail verdict per column check.  print method renders colour-coded cli summary.

title: Validate creel survey data frames — validate_creel_data
code: validate_creel_data(   counts = NULL,   interviews = NULL,   na_threshold = 0.1,   date_range = c(as.Date("1970-01-01"), as.Date("2100-12-31")) )

title: Validate creel survey data frames — validate_creel_data
text: counts data frame count (effort) observations, NULL skip. interviews data frame interview observations, NULL skip. na_threshold Numeric scalar \([0, 1]\). Columns NA rate threshold receive "warn" status. Default 0.10. date_range length-2 Date vector giving earliest latest plausible dates. Default c(.Date("1970-01-01"), .Date("2100-12-31")).

title: Validate creel survey data frames — validate_creel_data
text: object class creel_data_validation - tibble columns: table input checked: "counts" "interviews". column Column name. check Short check label (e.g. "na_rate", "negative_values", "type"). status "pass", "warn", "fail". detail Human-readable detail string.

title: Validate creel survey data frames — validate_creel_data
text: Checks performed every column: Type check - column class reported. NA rate - warns \(>\) na_threshold (default 0.10) values NA. Additional checks based detected column role: Date columns - values must fall within date_range (defaults 1970-01-01 - 2100-12-31); warns future dates. Numeric columns - warns value negative (effort/count \(\ge 0\)). Character/factor columns - warns value empty string.

title: Validate creel survey data frames — validate_creel_data
code: counts <- data.frame(   date     = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   count    = c(10L, NA_integer_) ) interviews <- data.frame(   date      = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   fish_kept = c(2L, -1L),   species   = c("walleye", "") ) res <- validate_creel_data(counts, interviews) print(res) #>  #> ── Creel Data Validation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> 15 pass | 3 warn | 0 fail #>  #>  #> ── Table: counts ── #>  #> ✔ date #> ✔ type: class: Date #> ✔ na_rate: 0 / 2 NA (0%) #> ✔ date_range: all within 1970-01-01 - 2100-12-31 #> ✔ day_type #> ✔ type: class: character #> ✔ na_rate: 0 / 2 NA (0%) #> ✔ empty_strings: none #> ! count #> ✔ type: class: integer #> ⚠ na_rate: 1 / 2 NA (50%) #> ✔ negative_values: none #>  #>  #> ── Table: interviews ── #>  #> ✔ date #> ✔ type: class: Date #> ✔ na_rate: 0 / 2 NA (0%) #> ✔ date_range: all within 1970-01-01 - 2100-12-31 #> ! fish_kept #> ✔ type: class: integer #> ✔ na_rate: 0 / 2 NA (0%) #> ⚠ negative_values: 1 negative value(s) #> ! species #> ✔ type: class: character #> ✔ na_rate: 0 / 2 NA (0%) #> ⚠ empty_strings: 1 empty string(s) #>

title: Validate a proposed creel survey design against sample size targets — validate_design
text: N_h Named numeric vector. Total available sampling days per stratum. ybar_h Numeric vector (length N_h). Pilot mean effort per day per stratum. s2_h Numeric vector (length N_h). Pilot variance effort per stratum. n_proposed Named integer vector (length N_h). Proposed sampling days per stratum. cv_target Numeric scalar. Target CV effort estimate. type Character. One "effort" "cpue". Default "effort". cv_catch Numeric scalar. Required type = "cpue". cv_effort Numeric scalar. Required type = "cpue". rho Numeric scalar. Correlation catch effort. Default 0.

what: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: Performs Two One-Sided Tests (TOST) determine incomplete trip CPUE estimates statistically equivalent complete trip estimates within specified threshold. Returns validation results recommendations whether incomplete trips appropriate estimation given dataset.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
code: validate_incomplete_trips(   design,   catch,   effort,   by = NULL,   variance = "taylor",   conf_level = 0.95,   truncate_at = 0.5 )

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: design creel_design object interviews attached via add_interviews. Must include trip_status field distinguish complete incomplete trips. catch Bare column name catch data (supports tidy evaluation) effort Bare column name effort data (supports tidy evaluation) Optional tidy selector grouping variables. provided, performs TOST group independently. Overall validation passes overall test group tests pass equivalence. variance Character string specifying variance estimation method. Options: "taylor" (default), "bootstrap", "jackknife". Passed estimate_catch_rate. conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals (default: 0.95). Passed estimate_catch_rate. truncate_at Numeric minimum trip duration (hours) incomplete trip estimation. Default 0.5 hours (30 minutes). Passed estimate_catch_rate MOR estimator.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: creel_tost_validation S3 object (list) components: overall_test: List TOST results overall comparison (p_lower, p_upper, equivalence_passed, diff_estimate, equivalence_bounds) group_tests: Data frame per-group TOST results (present grouped estimation) equivalence_threshold: Numeric threshold used (e.g., 0.20 ±20\ passed: Logical indicating overall groups (applicable) passed equivalence recommendation: Character string usage recommendation metadata: List complete incomplete trip estimates, standard errors, confidence intervals, sample sizes

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: function estimates CPUE separately complete trips (using ratio--means) incomplete trips (using mean--ratios) via estimate_catch_rate. performs TOST test equivalence within specified threshold. Equivalence bounds calculated ±threshold * complete_trip_estimate. difference variance estimated using delta method: Var(complete - incomplete) = Var(complete) + Var(incomplete), assuming independence two samples. Sample size requirements: least 10 complete trips 10 incomplete trips required stable variance estimation TOST. function errors either sample size insufficient.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: package option tidycreel.equivalence_threshold controls equivalence threshold (default: 0.20 = ±20\ bounds equivalence ±threshold * complete_trip_estimate. example, default 20\ equivalence bounds 1.6 2.4 fish/hour. Users can set custom threshold: options(tidycreel.equivalence_threshold = 0.15)

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: TOST (Two One-Sided Tests) statistically appropriate method proving similarity two estimates. Unlike traditional hypothesis testing tests difference, TOST tests null hypothesis estimates differ threshold. Equivalence concluded one-sided tests reject null (p-values < 0.05). two tests : H0: complete - incomplete <= -threshold vs H1: complete - incomplete > -threshold H0: complete - incomplete >= threshold vs H1: complete - incomplete < threshold tests must reject (p < 0.05) equivalence. ensures estimates "close enough" considered equivalent practical purposes.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
text: provided, function performs TOST group independently overall (ungrouped) data. validation passes tests pass equivalence. conservative approach prevents overlooking group-specific bias masked overall equivalence.

title: Validate incomplete trip estimates using TOST equivalence testing — validate_incomplete_trips
code: # Create design with both complete and incomplete trips calendar <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04")),   day_type = rep(c("weekday", "weekend"), each = 2) ) design <- creel_design(calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)  set.seed(123) interviews <- data.frame(   date = as.Date(rep(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02", "2024-06-03", "2024-06-04"), each = 25)),   catch_total = rpois(100, lambda = 6),   hours_fished = runif(100, min = 2, max = 4),   trip_status = rep(c("complete", "incomplete"), each = 50),   trip_duration = runif(100, min = 2, max = 4) )  design_with_interviews <- add_interviews(design, interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   trip_status = trip_status,   trip_duration = trip_duration ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> Warning: 1 interview has zero catch. #> ℹ Zero catch may be valid (skunked) or indicate missing data. #> ℹ Added 100 interviews: 50 complete (50%), 50 incomplete (50%)  # Validate incomplete trips result <- validate_incomplete_trips(design_with_interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished ) print(result) #>  #> ── TOST Equivalence Validation Results ───────────────────────────────────────── #> Threshold: ±20% of complete trip estimate #> ✔ Validation PASSED #>  #> Recommendation: Validation passed: Safe to use incomplete trips for CPUE #> estimation in this dataset #>  #>  #> ── Overall Test ── #>  #> Complete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 2.046 #> Incomplete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 1.969 #> Difference: 0.077 #> Equivalence bounds: [-0.409, 0.409] #> TOST p-values: p_lower = 0.0049, p_upper = 0.0359 #> ✔ Overall equivalence: PASSED #>    # Grouped validation result_grouped <- validate_incomplete_trips(design_with_interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished,   by = day_type ) #> Warning: ! Only 0.0% of interviews are complete trips (threshold: 10%) #> ℹ Pollock et al. recommends >=10% complete trips for valid estimation #> ℹ Consider use_trips='diagnostic' to validate incomplete trip estimates print(result_grouped) #>  #> ── TOST Equivalence Validation Results ───────────────────────────────────────── #> Threshold: ±20% of complete trip estimate #> ✔ Validation PASSED #>  #> Recommendation: Validation passed: Safe to use incomplete trips for CPUE #> estimation in this dataset (overall and all groups passed equivalence) #>  #>  #> ── Overall Test ── #>  #> Complete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 2.046 #> Incomplete trips: n = 50, CPUE = 1.969 #> Difference: 0.077 #> Equivalence bounds: [-0.409, 0.409] #> TOST p-values: p_lower = 0.0049, p_upper = 0.0359 #> ✔ Overall equivalence: PASSED #>  #>  #> ── Per-Group Tests ── #>  #> 1/1 group passed equivalence #>   # Custom equivalence threshold options(tidycreel.equivalence_threshold = 0.15) # 15% threshold result_custom <- validate_incomplete_trips(design_with_interviews,   catch = catch_total,   effort = hours_fished )

what: Generate a validation summary report — validation_report
title: Generate a validation summary report — validation_report
text: Runs validate_creel_data() counts /interviews, aggregates results human-readable summary tibble (one row per table x check type), optionally detects unrecognised species values via standardize_species().

title: Generate a validation summary report — validation_report
code: validation_report(   counts = NULL,   interviews = NULL,   species_col = NULL,   na_threshold = 0.1,   date_range = c(as.Date("1970-01-01"), as.Date("2100-12-31")) )

title: Generate a validation summary report — validation_report
text: counts data frame count (effort) observations, NULL. interviews data frame interview observations, NULL. species_col Character scalar. non-NULL interviews provided, calls standardize_species() column appends species_coverage row showing fraction rows successfully matched AFS code. Default NULL (species check). na_threshold Passed validate_creel_data(). Default 0.10. date_range Passed validate_creel_data(). Default c(.Date("1970-01-01"), .Date("2100-12-31")).

title: Generate a validation summary report — validation_report
text: object class creel_validation_report - data frame columns: table Source table: "counts", "interviews", "species". check Check type (e.g. "na_rate", "date_range"). n_pass Number columns "pass" status. n_warn Number columns "warn" status. n_fail Number columns "fail" status. detail Comma-separated list flagged columns, "ok".

title: Generate a validation summary report — validation_report
text: returned object creel_validation_report - data frame custom print method renders colour-coded cli summary. can exported write_estimates().

title: Generate a validation summary report — validation_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ counts <- data.frame(   date     = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   day_type = c("weekday", "weekend"),   count    = c(10L, NA_integer_) ) interviews <- data.frame(   date      = as.Date(c("2024-06-01", "2024-06-02")),   fish_kept = c(2L, -1L),   species   = c("walleye", "") ) rpt <- validation_report(counts, interviews, species_col = "species") print(rpt) } # }

what: Export creel survey estimates to a file — write_estimates
title: Export creel survey estimates to a file — write_estimates
text: Writes creel_estimates creel_summary object CSV xlsx file. CSV, three-line comment block prepended containing estimation method, variance method, confidence level, generation timestamp. xlsx, data written directly (Excel support comment rows).

title: Export creel survey estimates to a file — write_estimates
code: write_estimates(   x,   path,   format = c("auto", "csv", "xlsx"),   overwrite = FALSE,   ... )

title: Export creel survey estimates to a file — write_estimates
text: x creel_estimates creel_summary object. path File path output. extension (.csv .xlsx) determines format; alternatively, use format argument override. format One "csv" (default) "xlsx". "csv", comment header prepended. "xlsx", writexl::write_xlsx() used behind rlang::check_installed() guard. overwrite Logical; FALSE (default) error raised path already exists. ... Currently unused; reserved future arguments.

title: Export creel survey estimates to a file — write_estimates
text: path, returned invisibly.

title: Export creel survey estimates to a file — write_estimates
text: CSV format — output file begins comment lines starting # record survey metadata:   lines can skipped reading back utils::read.csv(path, comment.char = "#"). xlsx format — data written without comment header since Excel natively support comment rows. Row 1 column headers.
code: # Survey estimates — tidycreel # Method: Total Effort | Taylor linearization | 95% CI # Generated: 2024-06-15 09:32:11 UTC Estimate,SE,CI Lower,CI Upper,N 372.5,13.18,343.8,401.2,14

title: Export creel survey estimates to a file — write_estimates
code: data("example_counts") data("example_interviews") cal <- unique(example_counts[, c("date", "day_type")]) design <- suppressWarnings(   creel_design(cal, date = date, strata = day_type) # nolint ) design <- suppressWarnings(add_counts(design, example_counts)) design <- suppressWarnings(   add_interviews(     design, example_interviews,     catch = catch_total, effort = hours_fished, trip_status = trip_status   ) ) #> ℹ No `n_anglers` provided — assuming 1 angler per interview. #> ℹ Pass `n_anglers = <column>` to use actual party sizes for angler-hour #>   normalization. #> ℹ Added 22 interviews: 17 complete (77%), 5 incomplete (23%) eff <- suppressWarnings(estimate_effort(design))  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write_estimates(eff, tmp)  # Read back (skipping comment lines) out <- utils::read.csv(tmp, comment.char = "#") out #>   Estimate    SE CI.Lower CI.Upper  N #> 1    372.5 13.18    343.8    401.2 14

title: R Package GitHub Review Prompt
text: Review git diff R package, prioritizing GitHub delivery. Focus : Messages via cli, errors via rlang::abort(). Return types stable (tibble vs data.frame), attributes preserved. unexpected side effects (options, RNG, locale, working dir, connections). Use withr:: guards .exit(). Function signatures defaults consistent (including ...). S3/S4/R6/Rcpp compatibility; stable classes. Deprecations noted lifecycle (CRAN policy needed). Handles length-0/1, recycling, NA/NaN/Inf/NULL, factors vs character, UTF-8, Date/POSIXct + TZ. Tidy-eval safety ({{ }}, quosures). File paths portable (normalizePath(), tempfile()). Avoid accidental copies (copy--modify). Use preallocation. data.table -reference (:=) vs unintended copies. Parallel code portable (PSOCK Windows). Numeric tolerances appropriate. Invariants (classes/attributes) preserved. .onLoad/.onAttach mutate user state. Options/env vars namespaced. Add/adjust tests 0/1/NA/factors/TZ/locale/encoding. Snapshot outputs (CLI, printing). Error class assertions. Optional vdiffr plots. roxygen2 docs complete (@param/@return/@examples/@export). NAMESPACE regenerated. README install section (pak, remotes) current. NEWS.md tag changes. pkgdown builds. Minimal DESCRIPTION. CI green across OSes. Lints (lintr, styler) pass.

title: tidycreel 1.3.0 (2026-04-08)
text: estimate_catch_rate() now accepts estimator = "mortr" truncated mean--ratios (MORtr), applies truncate_at mandatory threshold labels method "mean--ratios-truncated-cpue". makes truncation behaviour explicit distinguishable plain MOR. estimate_catch_rate() gains targeted argument (default TRUE). Setting targeted = FALSE excludes zero-catch trips MOR/MORtr estimation — statistically appropriate approach non-targeted (incidental) species trips zero catch. cli_warn() fires targeted = TRUE >70% trips zero catch. compare_variance() computes Taylor linearization vs. replicate (bootstrap jackknife) standard errors side--side creel_estimates object, divergence flag cli_warn() two SEs differ configurable threshold (default 10%). adjust_nonresponse() applies nonresponse weighting creel_design scaling stratum weights inverse observed response rates. Returns adjusted creel_design nonresponse_diagnostics attribute detailing per-stratum rates adjustments (Armstrong & Overton 1977; Pollock et al. 1994).

title: tidycreel 1.3.0 (2026-04-08)
text: +56 new tests covering mortr, targeted, compare_variance(), adjust_nonresponse(). Total: 2132 PASS.

title: tidycreel 1.2.0 (2026-04-08)
text: summary.creel_estimates() converts estimate object creel_summary human-readable column names (Estimate, SE, CI Lower, CI Upper, N). Includes print.creel_summary() .data.frame.creel_summary() methods. Works effort, CPUE, harvest rate, total catch, grouped variants. flag_outliers() identifies extreme values numeric column using Tukey’s IQR fence (k = 1.5 default). Returns input data frame is_outlier, outlier_reason, fence_low, fence_high columns appended, emits cli summary flagged rows. Handles n < 4, empty input, zero-row data frames gracefully. ggplot2::autoplot.creel_estimates() produces point--errorbar plot creel_estimates object. Ungrouped estimates show single point confidence interval; grouped estimates show one point per group level, colour-coded. ggplot2::autoplot.creel_schedule() produces monthly tile calendar creel_schedule object. Sampled dates coloured day type (weekday blue / weekend red); unsampled dates shown grey. Multiple months displayed vertically stacked facet panels.

title: tidycreel 1.2.0 (2026-04-08)
text: Single-PSU strata now produce structured, actionable error instead opaque survey:::onestrat message. error names problematic stratum suggests increasing sampling rate combining sparse strata. Fixed bug aerial-glmm vignette downstream estimation chunk example_aerial_interviews paired GLMM design (built example_aerial_glmm_counts). chunk now uses correct matching dataset (example_aerial_counts + example_aerial_interviews).

title: tidycreel 1.2.0 (2026-04-08)
text: ggplot2 added Imports support new autoplot.* methods.

title: tidycreel 1.1.0 (2026-04-02)
text: generate_count_times() adds three sampling strategies allocating interview periods within survey day: random, systematic, fixed-interval. Supports seed argument reproducibility; returns creel_schedule object compatible write_schedule(). survey-scheduling vignette now covers full pre- post-season planning workflow: generate_count_times() validate_design(), check_completeness(), season_summary().

title: tidycreel 1.1.0 (2026-04-02)
text: GitHub issue templates now use structured forms blank_issues_enabled: false, routing -questions GitHub Discussions keep answers searchable users. CONTRIBUTING.md rewritten current workflow guidance, contribution types, community norms v1.x release line.

title: tidycreel 1.0.0 (2026-03-31)
text: Launched pkgdown documentation site https://chrischizinski.github.io/tidycreel custom Bootstrap 5 theme, full function reference index (46 exports + 15 datasets), workflow-driven navbar. Added GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow deploy pkgdown site automatically every push main.
